Items.Title: 让我们谈谈设计模式... | Season 9 of DevDiscuss is here! Let's talk about design patterns...
Items.Description:
开发团队的Ben Halpern

发表于在首映式中，我的共同主持人@ridhwana和我专注于使用设计模式来改进您与@lydiahallie和@addyosmani一起构建 Web 应用程序的方式——这本书是新书Learning Patterns的合著者。

我们希望您喜欢收听 DevDiscuss 的第 9 季首播：这个节目我们解决了影响我们作为开发人员生活的紧迫问题。

DevDiscuss Season 9 is here! 🎉

Items.Title: 18,000 位关注者 AMA 🙀 | 18,000 followers AMA 🙀
Items.Description:
亚当克罗克特

发表于你们不知怎么做到的，让我觉得自己很有名，从地板上提高了我的自尊心，并推动我通过工作面试，赋予我魅力和魅力。

我一直在学习如何与其他人相处，该说什么以及如何说，这完全取决于技术写作和你惊人的追随者。

如果你不太了解我，这里有几件事我从未在这里告诉过任何人。

  • 我出生在一个悲伤的世界，几个月大时从父母身边带走并被寄养，我的母亲患有肺痨病和穿着不好的感觉
  • 我一直觉得自己像个陌生的孩子，也许我永远不会有太多的数据，但我从不放弃，我想在某件事上做到最好
  • 那年我认识了我的女朋友，她现在是我孩子的妈妈
  • 大学毕业后，在艰难的时期，我创办了一家企业并经营了 3.5 年，为当地政府和小企业制作网站，它让我们大部分时间都维持生计
  • 过了一会儿，我决定我需要一份稳定的工作，宝宝要来了！ 我在linkedin上问了我在戴森IT能找到的最资深的人，他说进来面试（要东西，这很重要！）
  • 在戴森工作了几年后，我来到了这里，成为了我最喜欢的开发人员职位。 我看到的堆栈比大多数人看到的都要多，我会专业地告诉你它如何改进以及我发现了哪些安全漏洞。
  • 我的儿子是自闭症，部分语言，他的生活会和我完全不同，我担心我离开后他会发生什么，我从无到有过着成功的生活，现在他在一个可爱的家中安顿下来，他是如此被爱，但他没有像我这样的选择更多，他就是他自己，这不应该吓到我，但确实如此。

所以这就是我现在，非常感谢你们，我会继续努力想你们想听的东西！ 我们一起成长💗

安东尼奥·吉戈 - 普里亚斯利瓦斯塔瓦 - 桑多尔达戈 - Jun 19 '21 21 年 6 月 19 日



原文:

Adam Crockett

Posted on May 11

Somehow you guys did it you made me feel famous, raised my self esteem from the floor and pushed me through job interviews granting me charm and charisma.

I have been learning how to behave around other humans what to say and how to say it and it's all down to technical writing and your amazing following.

If you dont know me so well, here's a few things I have never told anyone on here.

  • I was born into a sad kind of world, taken from my parents at just a few months old and placed into foster care, my mother suffered/s from phycosis and a bad dress sense
  • I always felt like an alien kid and perhaps I would never amount to much after all that's what the statistics said, but I never ever give up, I wanted to be the best at something
  • at 18 I had to leave and find a place to live, I fell into University studying art because it was a kind of therapy, I learned graphic design and wrote a portfolio website or two but for paintings not code
  • I met my girlfriend that year who is now the mother of my child
  • after university, times where tough, I started a business and ran it for 3.5 years making websites for local government and small businesses, it kept us afloat most of the time
  • After a while I decided that I needed a stable job, the baby was coming! I asked on linkedin the most senior person I could find in Dyson IT, he said come on in and have an interview (ask for stuff, it's important!)
  • After a successful few years at Dyson I landed here in my absolutely favourite position being kind of a developer. I see more stacks than most will ever see and I get to professionaly tell you how it can improve and what security flaws I find.
  • my son is Autistic and partially verbal, he will have a very different life than me and I worry what will happen to him when I'm gone, I had a successful life from nothing, now he's setup in a lovely home, he is so loved but he hasn't got a choice like me to be more, he is exactly who he is, that should not scare me but it does.

So that's me right now, thank you all so much and I will continue to try to think of stuff you want to hear about! We grow together 💗

Antonio Djigo - Jun 14 '21

Priya Srivastava - Jun 19 '21

Sandor Dargo - Jun 19 '21

Items.Title: [BADGE.AR] 根据您的成就创建 NFT | [BADGE.AR] Create NFTs from your Achievements
Items.Description:
根据您的成就创建 NFT。

当我们说“成就”时，我们的意思是您可以连接您的帐户并根据该社区或提供商给予您的奖励创建 NFT。 例如，您可以添加您的 Steam 帐户并从您的 Steam 成就创建 NFT。 换句话说，您可以从您的游戏会话中铸造 NFT。

对于这个 MVP，唯一可用的提供商是 Steam，但我们计划在未来添加其他类型的提供商。 这可能会打开根据您在其他平台/活动中的成就创建 NFT 的可能性。

为了实现这一点，我们使用appwirte作为后端来管理
我们的前端和连接到 cardano 区块链的节点之间的交互。

启发我们这样做的想法是建立一个去中心化的地方来存储我们来自不同平台的成就、徽章、奖励，并使这些成就不仅仅是我们获得它们的平台中的徽章。

      原文:

      Create NFTs from your Achievements.

      BadgeAr is a place where you can transform your awards, trophies, and badges into a digital token (NFT).

      When we say "achievements" we mean that you can connect your accounts and create NFTs from awards given to you by that community or provider. For example, you can add you Steam account and create a NFT from your Steam achievements. In other words, you can mint NFTs from your gaming sessions.

      For this MVP, the only abailable provider is Steam, but we plan to add other kind of providers in the future. This could open the posibility to create NFTs from your achievements in other platforms / activities.

      In order to achieve this, we use appwirte as a backend to manage
      the interaction between our frontend and our nodes connected to the cardano blockchain.

      What inspired us to do this was the idea of having a decentralized place to store our achievements, badges, awards from different platforms and to make these achievements be more than just a badge within the platforms where we get them.

Items.Title: 您会向 JS 全栈开发人员推荐哪种“第二”语言？ | What "second" language would you recommend a JS Full-Stack Developer?
Items.Description:
克里斯蒂安·科扎拉

发表于嘿朋友们！ 很高兴见到你 ：）
我主要作为前端开发人员工作，但已经获得了一些使用 JavaScript 进行后端开发的经验——主要是在辅助项目中。

除了 JavaScript，您选择的服务器端语言是什么？ 或者你会推荐我什么语言？



原文:

Christian Kozalla

Posted on May 11

Hey friends! Nice to meet you :)
I primarily work as a frontend developer, but have already gained some experience in backend development with JavaScript - mostly in side-projects.

Recently, I'm feeling kinda interested in learning another server-side language for web development. Plus, I want to increase my backend skills in general.

Apart from JavaScript, what is your server-side language of choice? Or what language would you recommend me?

Items.Title: 在 Remix 中添加顺风排版插件 | Adding tailwind typography plugin in Remix
Items.Description:
现在我们向 Remix 网站添加了一些降价文件，我们看到了实际内容没有呈现标题的问题。

这是因为 Tailwind 并不真正知道将其渲染为什么。
因此，我们可以使用Tailwind Typography 插件来帮助解决这个问题。

在 Remix 中安装 Tailwind Typography 插件

这个插件的安装非常简单，因为我们只需要使用 NPM 安装依赖项：

 npm install -D @tailwindcss/typography

完成后，我们需要在 tailwind 配置文件中注册它。 该文件位于我们项目的根目录下，名为： tailwind.config.js 。

在此文件中添加插件：

 module . exports = { content : [ ' ./app/**/*.{ts,tsx,jsx,js} ' ], theme : { extend : {}, }, plugins : [ require ( ' @tailwindcss/typography ' )], };

我们可以将散文类添加到我们想要使用这个插件的任何元素上。

在我们的例子中，让我们将它添加到我们的根文件中，以便我们可以将它用于我们的降价文件。

 export default function App () { return ( < html lang = ' en ' className = ' h-full ' > ... < body className = ' h-full p-4 prose ' > < Outlet /> ... < /body > < /html > ); }

注意正文上的prose类。 这就是使它活跃的原因。
如果我们现在运行我们的网站并查看降价渲染的帖子，我们会看到它在运行。

您还可以在GitHub 上找到完整的代码。

感谢您的阅读，让我们联系吧！

感谢您阅读我的博客。 随时订阅我的电子邮件通讯并在Facebook或Twitter 上联系



原文:

Now that we added some markdown files to our Remix website, we saw the issue of the actual content not rendering the headings.

This is because Tailwind doesn't really know what to render this as.
Thus, we can use the Tailwind Typography plugin to help with this issue.

Installing the Tailwind Typography plugin in Remix

The installation of this plugin is super straightforward, as we simply have to install the dependency with NPM:

npm install -D @tailwindcss/typography

Once this is done, we need to register it in our tailwind config file. This file is located at the root of our project named: tailwind.config.js.

Inside this file add the plugin:

module.exports = { content: ['./app/**/*.{ts,tsx,jsx,js}'], theme: { extend: {}, }, plugins: [require('@tailwindcss/typography')],
};

We can add the prose class to any element we want to use this plugin on.

In our case, let's add it to our root file so we can use it for our markdown files.

export default function App() { return ( <html lang='en' className='h-full'> ... <body className='h-full p-4 prose'> <Outlet /> ... </body>
 </html>
 );
}

Note the prose class on the body. This is what will make it active.
If we now run our website and view a markdown rendered post, we see it in action.

You can also find the completed code on GitHub.

Thank you for reading, and let's connect!

Thank you for reading my blog. Feel free to subscribe to my email newsletter and connect on Facebook or Twitter

Items.Title: Flutter 发布 3.0.0 有什么新功能？ | Flutter release 3.0.0 What's new ?
Items.Description:
斯坦尼斯拉夫·伊林

发表于这次大会的消息之一是发布了 Flutter 3.0.0 版本，这次谷歌团队做了哪些准备？ 让我们弄清楚吧！

稳定支持 MacOS 和 Linux

现在，flutter 已准备好在所有桌面平台上进行生产，Linux 和 macOS 已达到稳定并包含以下功能：

  • 级联菜单和对 macOS 系统菜单栏的支持
  • 全面支持所有桌面平台上的国际文本输入
  • 所有桌面平台的可访问性
  • 弃用 Windows 7/8 进行开发

新的 Google Pixel 手机使用了它，现在 Flutter 应用程序也支持它。

在这里你可以查看 Flutter 团队关于如何在你的 Flutter 应用程序中实现新的 Material 3 api 的文章

可折叠手机支持

什么手机？ 是那些能够展开的手机

如果您一直在关注 Flutter 社区的发布，您可能已经注意到这个存储库flutter/pinball

是的，这个惊人的游戏是在 Flutter 上使用火焰制作的
令我惊讶的是，Flutter 团队在这次更新中非常关注游戏的制作

老实说，这很棒。 这个动作展示了 Flutter 是一个可以制作休闲游戏的强大框架的另一个例子。

而在本次更新中，Flutter Team 还发布了休闲游戏工具包

它旨在让您更轻松地创建简单游戏并使用 Google 服务支持它们。

网站说我们可以

通过对广告、应用内购买、Firebase、Play 服务和 Game Center 等服务的预构建集成来加快游戏开发

我想知道这会发生什么，以及 Flutter 上的游戏故事将如何发展。 我什至想在 Flutter 上写一个游戏，现在就给自己发火😁

那么，如果没有 linter 更新，flutter 更新怎么会发生呢？ 是的，对于 Flutter 上的新应用程序， flutter_lints版本将自动为 2.0.0。

现在 Flutter in web 可以在支持它的浏览器中自动使用 ImageDecoder API。

更多在这里

结论

我很高兴添加了许多新功能，这些功能有助于我最喜欢的框架的扩展和普及

如果我没有提到更新中的任何内容，请写在评论中
感谢您的阅读并继续关注！😘



原文:

Stanislav Ilin

Posted on May 12

Google I/O is happening now. One of the news from this conference is release a Flutter version 3.0.0 What has the google team prepared this time? Let's figure it out!

Stable support for MacOS and Linux

Now flutter is ready for production on all desktop platforms Linux and macOS have reached stable and include this features:

  • Cascading menus and support for the macOS system menu bar
  • Full support for international text input on all desktop platforms
  • Accessibility on all desktop platforms
  • Universal binaries by default on macOS
  • Deprecating Windows 7/8 for development

Material Design 3(Material You)

Material You is new design language update. New Google Pixel phones used it and now Flutter applications is support it too.

Material You included features like dynamic color, an updated color system and typography, updates to many components, and new visual effects introduced in Android 12 like a new touch ripple design and a stretch overscroll effect.

Here you can check article from the Flutter Team about how to implement new Material 3 api in your Flutter application

Foldable phone support

Another feature in new Flutter release is a stable support for foldable phones. What phones? It is those phones that are able to unfold

MediaQuery now contains a list of DisplayFeatures, describing the bounds and states of device elements like hinges, folds, and cutouts.

Flutter Casual Games Toolkit

If you have been following the releases of the Flutter community, you may have noticed this repository flutter/pinball

Yes, this amazing game was made on Flutter using flame
I was surprised that the Flutter team paid a lot of attention to the production of games in this update

Honestly, it's wonderful. This action shows another example of the fact that Flutter is a powerful framework on which you can make casual games.

And in this update, Flutter Team also released the Casual Games Toolkit

It is designed to make it easier to create simple games and support them using Google services.

The website says that we can

Speed up your game's development with pre-built integrations for services like Ads, In-App Purchases, Firebase, Play Services, and Game Center

I wonder what will come of this and how the story of the games on Flutter will develop. I even wanted to write a game on Flutter and flame myself now 😁

flutter_lints updated to 2.0.0

Well, how could a flutter update happen without a linter updates? Yes, for new applications on Flutter, the flutter_lints version will be 2.0.0 automatically.

Image decoding and app lifecycles for Web

Now Flutter in web can automatically uses the ImageDecoder API in browsers that support it.

The new lifecycle API for Flutter web apps gives you the flexibility to control the bootstrap process of your Flutter app from the hosting HTML page. More here

Conclusion

The Flutter Team amazes with each of its new updates
I am glad that many new features are being added that contribute to the expansion and popularization of my favorite framework

Write in the comments if I didn't mention anything from the update
Thank you for reading and stay tuned!😘
Items.Author: frezyx

Items.Title: 快速提示：对 unix 套接字的存根请求 | Quick tip: stub requests to unix sockets
Items.Description:
瓦西里·波洛夫尼奥夫

发表于有时，应用程序不通过 Internet 套接字，而是通过 unix 套接字与外部服务通信：

 Excon . get ( "unix:///ping" , socket: "/tmp/unicorn.sock" )

要在测试中存根这样的请求，请使用 Webmock：

 stub_request ( :get , "http://unix/ping" ). to_return ( status: 200 )

马蒂亚斯·卡平蒂尼 - Apr 11 4月11日



原文:

Vasily Polovnyov

Posted on May 12

Sometimes, an application communicates with an external service not via Internet sockets, but via unix sockets:

Excon.get("unix:///ping", socket: "/tmp/unicorn.sock")

To stub requests like this in tests, use Webmock:

stub_request(:get, "http://unix/ping").to_return(status: 200)

Matias Carpintini - Apr 11
Items.Author: vasily

Items.Title: 16 个跨浏览器兼容 Web 开发的免费资源 | 16 Free Resources For Cross Browser Compatible Web Development
Items.Description:
拥有数百种开源工具、库和框架，以及大量在线教程，您可以轻松上手并很快成为一名成功的 Web 开发人员。 虽然，无论您的 web 应用程序有多大或多小，您都不能忽视浏览器的多样性。 无论您的目标受众是什么，您都应该始终致力于开发一个跨浏览器兼容的网站。 在本文中，我们将讨论 16 个免费资源，它们对初学者和高级 Web 开发人员都非常有用，可用于创建跨浏览器兼容的网站或 Web 应用程序。

嘿，你知道Scala 测试吗？现在在最可靠和可扩展的 CMS 测试云上测试 Scala 网站。 运行使用 $Scala 构建的移动和 Web 应用程序的端到端手动或自动跨浏览器测试。

该框架基于 HTML、CSS 和 JavaScript。 它于 2011 年设计，是用于开发具有加速页面加载速度的响应式网站的最常用框架。

由 Google 于 2012 年开发的 Angular JS，俗称 Angular，是迄今为止最流行的基于 JavaScript 的框架。 它是开发动态单页 Web 应用程序的理想选择，有助于根据数据中所做的更改更新您的站点。

就像 Angular 一样，它也是基于组件驱动的结构。

这也是一个基于 Model View Presentation 框架工作的 JavaScript 库。 它于 2016 年发布，使用 JavaScript 和 RESTful Web API，由于轻量级且仅依赖 JavaScript，该框架变得非常流行。

使用 CSS、HTML 和 Javascript 开发。 它是一个用于 Web 开发的开源轻量级文本编辑器。 该工具由 Adob​​e 设计，独立于平台并由 MIT 许可。 你喜欢括号吗？ 如果你是，那么这里有一些在编码时使用的最有用的括号扩展。

你知道语义 ui 测试吗 - 一种在线测试工具，可确保你的语义 UI 网页在所有移动设备上兼容，并且在所有移动屏幕尺寸上都具有响应性。

该框架由非营利组织 Django 基金会开发和维护。

这是另一个基于模型-视图-视图模型（MVVM）架构的开源框架。 它于 2011 年发布，并受到许多开发人员的喜爱，因为它通过结合最新的 ECMA 6 脚本和标准 Web 开发实践来实现可扩展的 Web 应用程序开发。

当您使用 Node JS 时，它是默认的包管理器。 它由一个在线软件包数据库组成，还包含一个命令行客户端。 它是一种用于维护版本控制的工具，您可以使用它来跟踪代码中所做的更改。 如果您的代码中出现了几乎无法修复的缺陷，Git 可用于返回到以前的版本并撤消更改。 对于 Web 开发，版本控制非常重要，而 Git 是业内最好的工具。

随着 Foundation 的引入，响应式设计变得更好。 它是一组框架，可以使用这些框架设计完全响应的漂亮模板、应用程序、网站和电子邮件。 一个可扩展且可靠的在线测试云，用于 Sencha Touch 网站的手动和自动化测试。 立即检查 — Sencha Touch 测试

它遵循模型视图控制器模式，并提供创建包含数据库的 Web 应用程序所需的一切。

它执行诸如代码编译、缩小、linting 以及单元测试等常见任务。 可以通过命令行界面使用 Grunt 并执行在文件中定义的自定义任务。 它通过 npm 分发并用 Node JS 编写。 迄今为止，Grunt 生态系统包含超过 5000 个插件，这些插件使 Web 开发更轻松、更快捷。

它还具有转换和捆绑任何资源或包的能力。 它将不兼容的源代码或库功能转换为您的浏览器可能兼容的浏览器支持的代码。 转译器经常被误认为是编译器，但它们有点不同。 他们处理源代码并仅返回更坚固的兼容源代码。 开发人员甚至可以自定义 Babel 以更好地适应他们。

这将检查浏览器是否支持代码并给你一个“假”或“真”的结果。

上述列表并非基于排名或受欢迎程度。 除此之外，还有无穷无尽的资源可以帮助您创建强大且完全响应的 Web 应用程序。 所以，快点，选择你的选择，开始在万维网的惊人领​​域。



原文:

Web development has never been the way it is currently. With more than hundreds of open source tools, libraries, and frameworks, along with lots of online tutorials, one can easily get started and very soon become a successful web developer. Although, irrespective of how big or small your webapp is, you cannot afford to neglect browser diversity. No matter what your target audience is, you should always aim to develop a website that is cross browser compatible. In this article, we shall discuss 16 free resources that are extremely helpful for a beginner as well as an advanced web developer for creating a cross browser compatible website or web application.

Hey, do you know Scala Testing — Now test Scala websites on the most reliable and scalable CMS testing cloud. Run end to end manual or automated cross browser testing of mobile and web apps build using $Scala.

1. Node.js

Built around the latest v8 engine of Chrome, Node.js is an open-source JavaScript-based framework that runs across multiple platforms. Initially released for Linux in 2009, Node JS is the perfect based framework for developing single-page web applications.

2. Bootstrap

This framework is based on HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. Designed in 2011, it is the most used framework for developing responsive websites with accelerated page loading speed.

3. AngularJS

Developed by Google in 2012, Angular JS, commonly known as Angular is the most popular framework based on JavaScript to date. It is ideal for developing dynamic single page web applications that help to update your site based on the changes made in data.

4. React JS

Released in 2013 by Facebook and maintained by a community of users and developers, React is ideal for developing single-page web and mobile applications. Just like Angular, it is also based on a component-driven structure.

5. Backbone.JS

This is also a JavaScript library that works based on the Model View Presentation framework. Released in 2016, it uses JavaScript along with RESTful web APIs, this framework has become quite popular due to being lightweight and its sole dependency on JavaScript.

6. Brackets

Developed using CSS, HTML, and Javascript. It is an open-source lightweight text editor meant for web development. Designed by Adobe, the tool is platform-independent and licensed by MIT. Are you fond of Brackets? If you are, then here are some of the most useful Brackets extension to use when coding.

Do you know semantic ui testing — An online testing tool to ensure that your Semantic UI webpages are compatible across all mobile devices and are responsive across all mobile screen sizes.

7. Django

Based on Python and following the model-view-template architectural pattern, Django is an open-source framework that makes developing a rapid and clean design. The framework is developed and maintained by the Django foundation, a non-profit organization.

8. Ember.js

This is another open-source framework that is based on Model-view-viewmodel(MVVM) architecture. It was released in 2011 and has been a favorite among many developers since it allows scalable web application development by incorporating the latest ECMA 6 script and standard web development practices.

9. npm

npm is a package manager used for programming in JavaScript. When you are using Node JS, it is the default package manager. It consists of an online database of packages and also contains a command-line client. Consisting of reusable packages, npm is the largest software registry in the world.

10. Git

Git is not related to development. It is a tool for maintaining version control using which you can track the changes made in your code. If some defect occurs in your code that is almost impossible to fix, Git can be used to go back to the previous version and undo the changes. For web development, version control is highly essential and Git is the best tool available in the industry.

11. Foundation

The responsive design got a whole lot better with the introduction of the Foundation. It is a group of frameworks using which, one can design beautiful templates, apps, websites, and emails that are fully responsive. The best feature of Foundation is that, its flexible, semantic and completely customizable with new resources being added regularly.

A scalable and reliable online testing cloud for both manual and automation testing of Sencha Touch websites. Check it now — Sencha Touch Testing

12. Ruby

Developed in the middle of 1990 by Yukihiro Matsumoto in Japan, Ruby is an effective and dynamic programming language that focuses on productivity and simplicity. It follows the model view controller pattern and provides everything needed to create a web application packed with the database.

13. Grunt

Grunt is a task runner based on JavaScript. Frequent tasks like code compilation, minification, linting as well as unit testing are performed by it. One can use Grunt by using a command-line interface and execute custom tasks that are defined in a file. It is distributed via npm and written in Node JS. To date, the Grunt ecosystem contains more than 5000 plugins that make web development easier and faster.

14.Webpack

Webpack is an open source bundler based on JavaScript. The main purpose of Webpack is to bundle JavaScript files that can be later, used in the browser. It also has the capability to transform and bundle any resource or package. Released in 2012, Webpack generates static assets that are dependent on the modules on which it is worked.

15. Bable.js

Babel is a transpiler and a brilliant one indeed. It converts incompatible source code or the library functionality into browser supported code which your browser may find compatible. Transpilers are often mistaken for compilers, but they are a bit different. They work on the source code and return a more sturdy compatible source code only. Developers can even customize Babel to suit them better.

16. Modernizr

Modernizr that can efficiently solve challenges faced in cross browser web development and based on test case scenarios, conditionally load JS or CSS files. Modernizr detects the CSS3 and HTML5 features supported by the browser of the end-user. Modernizr introduces Feature Detection. That will check whether the browser supports the code and gives you a “false” or “true” result.

The above-mentioned list is not based on ranking or popularity. Apart from these, there are endless resources that will help you create a robust and fully responsive web application. So, hurry up, choose your pick and get started in the amazing domain of the world wide web.

Items.Title: 我们将新功能命名为支持！ | OpenBB is Setting the Standard for Customer Support and Service in the FOSS Community. We Named our new Feature, Support!
Items.Description:
发布一个开源项目是一回事，而在工作场所采用开源解决方案则是另一回事。 在为用户提供一整套 MIT 许可的金融研究和公共股票分析工具时，OpenBB 希望他们放心，因为他们已经依赖的工具将在他们需要时继续为他们提供服务帮助，或者当事情莫名其妙地破裂时。

根据工作场所的类型，采用开源解决方案和平台可以很好地工作，直到他们不这样做。 如果一个在组织内整合 OSS 生态系统的人离开，那么其维护或改进现有系统的能力也会随之消失。 这是一场在后台深处进行的安静对话，但对网络安全风险的新担忧正在推动专有封闭软件和技术不可避免地消亡的时间表。 最终，开源会吃掉一切。

对于那些不熟悉该主题的人，这个简短的视频很好地解释了大型组织在采用 OSS 解决方案时面临的一般问题。

虽然目标不是一个完整的企业级解决方案，但关键目标是消除通常阻止用户提交问题的错误、缺陷、一般问题和建议的摩擦点。

注册或加入服务的障碍有多少次阻止您与他们的支持团队互动？ 您有多少次因为糟糕或不存在的用户支持而停止使用软件或服务？

为了开始支持我们的用户，我们为他们添加了一个全局命令来预填充表单，然后将其呈现以供最终提交。 无需再寻找位置来提问，也无需费力通过 GitHub 来打开问题。 任何人都可以从终端的任何地方请求支持。

 (🦋) /crypto/dd/ $ support -h usage: support [-c {generic,load,oi,active,change,nonzero,eb}] [--msg MSG [MSG ...]] [--type {bug,suggestion,question,generic}] [-h] Submit your support request optional arguments: -c {generic,load,oi,active,change,nonzero,eb}, --command {generic,load,oi,active,change,nonzero,eb} Command that needs support (default: None) --msg MSG [MSG ...], -m MSG [MSG ...] Message to send. Enclose it with double quotes (default: ) --type {bug,suggestion,question,generic}, -t {bug,suggestion,question,generic} Support ticket type (default: generic) -h, --help show this help message (default: False)

如果用户想要对提交的回复，他们可以包括他们选择的电子邮件地址。 剩下要做的就是审查、附上任何屏幕截图并提交！

下载 OpenBB 终端以获取一整套用 Python 编写的开源工具，用于金融和公共市场研究。

兼容 Mac、Windows、Linux、树莓派。

访问 GitHub 存储库以检查代码并给项目打个星！

在我们的 Discord 服务器上与来自世界各地的用户和贡献者聊天！

在 Twitter 上关注 OpenBB： @openbb_finance



原文:

It is one thing to publish an open-source project, and it is an entirely different animal to adopt open-source solutions for the workplace. When it comes to providing its users with a comprehensive suite of MIT-licensed financial research and public equities analysis tools, OpenBB wants them to take comfort in knowing that the tools they have grown to rely on will continue to be there for them when they need help, or when things break inexplicably.

Depending on the type of workplace, adopting open-source solutions and platforms can work great until they don't. If a person integrating the OSS ecosystem within an organization leaves, then so does its ability to maintain or improve the systems in place. It has been a quiet conversation taking place deep in the background, but new concerns with cybersecurity risks are advancing timelines for the inevitable demise of proprietary closed software and technology. Eventually, open-source eats everything.

For those unfamiliar with the topic, this short video does a good job of explaining the general problem large organizations face while adopting OSS solutions.

While the goal was not a complete enterprise-grade solution, the key objective was to remove points of friction ordinarily preventing users from submitting issues for bugs, deficiencies, general questions, and suggestions.

How many times has the barrier of signing-up, or in, to a service prevented you from interacting with their support team? How many times have you stopped using software or a service because of bad, or non-existent, user support?

To begin supporting our users, we added a global command for them to pre-populate a form and then presents it for final submission. No more hunting for a location to ask questions, or wading through GitHub to open an issue. OpenBB wants to reduce friction from participating in our global community of users, contributors, and maintainers.

Anyone can request support, from anywhere in the Terminal.

(🦋) /crypto/dd/ $ support -h

usage: support [-c {generic,load,oi,active,change,nonzero,eb}] [--msg MSG [MSG ...]] [--type {bug,suggestion,question,generic}] [-h]

Submit your support request

optional arguments:
  -c {generic,load,oi,active,change,nonzero,eb}, --command {generic,load,oi,active,change,nonzero,eb}
                        Command that needs support (default: None)
  --msg MSG [MSG ...], -m MSG [MSG ...]
                        Message to send. Enclose it with double quotes (default: )
  --type {bug,suggestion,question,generic}, -t {bug,suggestion,question,generic}
                        Support ticket type (default: generic)
  -h, --help            show this help message (default: False)

If a user would like a reply to the submission, they can include an email address of their choice. All that is left to do is review, attach any screenshots, and submit!

Download the OpenBB Terminal to get a comprehensive set of open-source tools, written in Python, for financial and public markets research.

Compatible with Mac, Windows, Linux, Raspberry Pi.

https://openbb.co

Visit the GitHub repo to inspect the code and give the project a star!

https://github.com/OpenBB-finance/OpenBBTerminal

Chat with users and contributors from around the world in our Discord server!

https://discord.com/invite/Up2QGbMKHY

Follow OpenBB on Twitter: @openbb_finance

Items.Title: Node.js 中的密码学 | Cryptography in Node.js
Items.Description:
玫瑰卡马尔爱

发表于链接到原始帖子
链接到视频

神秘的密码学学科是互联网的支柱。 没有它，数字世界就没有秘密和隐私。 作为开发人员，您不需要了解密码学中的数学知识，但它绝对是必不可少的

了解哈希、盐、密钥对、加密和签名等关键概念。

以下教程解释了基本的密码学概念，然后使用内置的Node.js 加密模块实现

它意味着切碎和混合，完美地描述了散列函数的作用。 它接受任意长度的输入值并输出固定长度的值。 哈希算法，

像SHA （安全散列算法）一样，从给定的输入生成一个随机的、唯一的、固定长度的字符串。 他们是


通常用于比较两个值（如密码）是否相等。
  • 相同的输入总是会产生相同的输出。
  • 计算速度快，但查找原始输入的计算成本很高
  • 碰撞概率小（唯一）
 Hello World -> {Hashing Function} => jibbrish(hashed stuff)

执行：

 const { createHash } = require ( ' crypto ' ) // Create a string hash function hash ( str ) { return createHash ( ' sha256 ' ). update ( str ). digest ( ' hex ' ) } // Compare two hashed passwords let password = ' hi-mom! ' const hash1 = hash ( password ) console . log ( hash1 ) // ... some time later password = ' hi-mom ' const hash2 = hash ( password ) const match = hash1 === hash2 console . log ( match ? ' good password ' : ' password does not match ' )

哈希非常适合使密码不可读，但因为它们总是产生相同的输出，所以它们不是很安全。 盐是在散列之前添加到输入的随机字符串。

这使得散列更加独特且更难猜测。

用户经常使用弱密码，例如“password123”。 当数据库被入侵时，攻击者可以轻松找到该值
通过搜索预先计算的常见哈希彩虹表来计算未加盐的哈希 - 加盐解决了这个问题。

  • 用于使哈希更难猜测
  • 在散列之前将随机字符串附加到输入
Hello World --(+random salt)--> {Hashing Function} => jibbrish(salted hashed stuff)

执行

const { scryptSync , randomBytes , timingSafeEqual } = require ( ' crypto ' ) function signup ( email , password ) { const salt = randomBytes ( 16 ). toString ( ' hex ' ) const hashedPassword = scryptSync ( password , salt , 64 ). toString ( ' hex ' ) const user = { email , password : ` ${ salt } : ${ hashedPassword } ` } users . push ( user ) return user } function login ( email , password ) { const user = users . find ( v => v . email === email ) const [ salt , key ] = user . password . split ( ' : ' ) const hashedBuffer = scryptSync ( password , salt , 64 ) const keyBuffer = Buffer . from ( key , ' hex ' ) const match = timingSafeEqual ( hashedBuffer , keyBuffer ) return match ? ' login success ' : ' login fail ' } const users = [] const user = signup ( ' foo@bar.com ' , ' pa$$word ' ) console . log ( user ) const result = login ( ' foo@bar.com ' , ' password ' ) console . log ( result )

要创建 HMAC，您需要拥有密钥，因此您可以验证真实性和

数据的发起者。 使用不同的密钥会产生不同的输出。

  • 将 HMAC 视为带有密码或密钥的哈希
  • 只有拥有密钥的人才能创建真实的哈希
Hello World --> {Hashing Function} => jibbrish(hashed stuff) | | |____ Shared Key ____|

执行：

 const { createHmac } = require ( ' crypto ' ) const password = ' super-secret! ' const message = ' 🎃 hello jack ' const hmac = createHmac ( ' sha256 ' , password ). update ( message ). digest ( ' hex ' ) console . log ( hmac )

加密是使消息保密（如哈希）的过程，同时允许使用适当的密钥对其进行可逆（解密）。 每次留言

被加密，它被随机化以产生不同的输出。 在对称加密中，相同的密钥


用于加密和解密消息。
  • 与哈希不同，相同的输入将产生不同的输出
  • 加密的消息可以用密钥反转
  • 用于加密和解密消息的相同密钥
Hello World --encrypt--> {jibbrish} --decrypt--> Hello World | | |______ Shared Key ______|

实现：通过创建密码在 Node 中执行对称加密。 加密还具有一个初始化向量 (IV) 来随机化模式，因此文本序列不会产生与前一个序列相同的输出。

 const { createCipheriv , randomBytes , createDecipheriv } = require ( ' crypto ' ) /// Cipher const message = ' i like turtles ' const key = randomBytes ( 32 ) const iv = randomBytes ( 16 ) const cipher = createCipheriv ( ' aes256 ' , key , iv ) /// Encrypt const encryptedMessage = cipher . update ( message , ' utf8 ' , ' hex ' ) + cipher . final ( ' hex ' ) console . log ( `Encrypted: ${ encryptedMessage } ` ) /// Decrypt const decipher = createDecipheriv ( ' aes256 ' , key , iv ) const decryptedMessage = decipher . update ( encryptedMessage , ' hex ' , ' utf-8 ' ) + decipher . final ( ' utf8 ' ) console . log ( `Deciphered: ${ decryptedMessage . toString ( ' utf-8 ' )} ` )

使用共享密钥进行加密有效，但问题是双方必须就密钥达成一致。 这在现实世界中是有问题的，因为通过网络共享既不实用也不安全。 这

解决方案是使用像RSA这样的算法来生成包含公钥和私钥的密钥对。

正如他们的名字所表明的，私钥应该保密，而公钥可以自由共享。
 Public Key <---- Mathematically Linked ----> Private Key

执行：

 const { generateKeyPairSync } = require ( ' crypto ' ) const { privateKey , publicKey } = generateKeyPairSync ( ' rsa ' , { modulusLength : 2048 , // the length of your key in bits publicKeyEncoding : { type : ' spki ' , // recommended to be 'spki' by the Node.js docs format : ' pem ' }, privateKeyEncoding : { type : ' pkcs8 ' , // recommended to be 'pkcs8' by the Node.js docs format : ' pem ' } }) console . log ( publicKey ) console . log ( privateKey )

非对称加密取决于两个密钥。 用公钥加密消息，用私钥解密。

每当您使用 HTTPS 与该网站建立加密连接时，都会在 Web 上使用非对称加密。 浏览器找到安装在网站上的 SSL 证书的公钥，该证书用于加密您发送的任何数据，然后私钥对其进行解密。

 Hello World --encrypt--> {jibbrish} --decrypt--> Hello World | | | | Public Key Private Key

执行：

 const { publicEncrypt , privateDecrypt } = require ( ' crypto ' ) const { publicKey , privateKey } = require ( ' ./keypair ' ) const encryptedData = publicEncrypt ( publicKey , Buffer . from ( secretMessage )) console . log ( encryptedData . toString ( ' hex ' )) const decryptedData = privateDecrypt ( privateKey , encryptedData ) console . log ( decryptedData . toString ( ' utf-8 ' ))

亚历克斯马尔多纳多索托 - 克里斯汀·金尼尔-奥尔曼 - Apr 18 4月18日



原文:

Rose Kamal Love

Posted on May 12

Link to Original Post
Link to Video

The mysterious discipline of cryptography is the backbone of the internet. Without it, there would be no secrets and no privacy in the digital world. As a developer, you don’t need to understand the math that goes into cryptography, but it’s absolutely essential to

know key concepts like hashes, salt, keypairs, encryption, and signing.

The follow tutorial explains essential cryptography concepts and implements then with the builtin Node.js crypto module

1. Hash

The word hash actually has culinary roots. It means to chop and mix and that perfectly describes what a hashing function does. It takes an input value of any length and outputs a fixed length value. Hashing algorithms,

like SHA (Secure Hashing Algorithm), produce a random, unique, fixed-length string from a given input. They are


often used to compare two values, like passwords, for equality.
  • The same input will always produce the same output.
  • Fast to compute, but computationally expensive to find the original input
  • Small probability of collision (unique) 
Hello World -> {Hashing Function} => jibbrish(hashed stuff)

Implementation:

const { createHash } = require('crypto') // Create a string hash function hash(str) { return createHash('sha256').update(str).digest('hex')
} // Compare two hashed passwords let password = 'hi-mom!'
const hash1 = hash(password)
console.log(hash1) // ... some time later password = 'hi-mom'
const hash2 = hash(password)
const match = hash1 === hash2 console.log(match ? 'good password' : 'password does not match')

2. Salt

Hashes are great for making passwords unreadable, but because they always produce the same output, they are not very secure. A salt is a random string that is added to the input before hashing.

This makes the hash more unique and harder to guess.

Users often to use weak passwords, like “password123”. When a database is compromised, the attacker can easily find the value
of an unsalted hash by searching precomputed rainbow table of common hashes - salting fixes this.

  • Used to make a hash harder to guess
  • Appends a random string to the input before hashing 
Hello World --(+random salt)--> {Hashing Function} => jibbrish(salted hashed stuff)

Implementation

const { scryptSync, randomBytes, timingSafeEqual } = require('crypto') function signup(email, password) { const salt = randomBytes(16).toString('hex') const hashedPassword = scryptSync(password, salt, 64).toString('hex') const user = { email, password: `${salt}:${hashedPassword}` } users.push(user) return user
} function login(email, password) { const user = users.find(v => v.email === email) const [salt, key] = user.password.split(':') const hashedBuffer = scryptSync(password, salt, 64) const keyBuffer = Buffer.from(key, 'hex') const match = timingSafeEqual(hashedBuffer, keyBuffer) return match ? 'login success' : 'login fail'
} const users = [] const user = signup('foo@bar.com', 'pa$$word') console.log(user) const result = login('foo@bar.com', 'password') console.log(result)

3. HMAC

HMAC is a keyed hash of data - like a hash with a password. To create a HMAC you need to have the key, therefore allowing you to verify both the authenticity and

originator of the data. Using a different key produces a different output.

  • Think of HMAC as a hash with a password or key
  • Only someone with the key can create an authentic hash 
Hello World  --> {Hashing Function} => jibbrish(hashed stuff)
               |                    |
               |____ Shared Key ____|

Implementation:

const { createHmac } = require('crypto') const password = 'super-secret!'
const message = '🎃 hello jack' const hmac = createHmac('sha256', password).update(message).digest('hex') console.log(hmac)

4. Symmetric Encryption

Encryption is the process making a message confidential (like a hash), while allowing it to be reversable (decrypted) with the proper key. Each time a message

is encrypted it is randomized to produce a different output. In symmetric encryption, the same key


is used to encrypt and decrypt the message.
  • The same input will produce a different output, unlike hashes
  • Encrypted message can be reversed with the key
  • Same key used to encrypt and decrypt message 
Hello World  --encrypt--> {jibbrish} --decrypt--> Hello World
                 |                        |
                 |______ Shared Key ______|

Implementation: Perform symmetric encryption in Node by creating a cipher. Encryption also has an initialization vector (IV) to randomize the pattern so a sequence of text won’t produce the same output as a previous sequence.

const { createCipheriv, randomBytes, createDecipheriv } = require('crypto') /// Cipher const message = 'i like turtles'
const key = randomBytes(32)
const iv = randomBytes(16) const cipher = createCipheriv('aes256', key, iv) /// Encrypt const encryptedMessage = cipher.update(message, 'utf8', 'hex') + cipher.final('hex')
console.log(`Encrypted: ${encryptedMessage}`) /// Decrypt const decipher = createDecipheriv('aes256', key, iv)
const decryptedMessage = decipher.update(encryptedMessage, 'hex', 'utf-8') + decipher.final('utf8')
console.log(`Deciphered: ${decryptedMessage.toString('utf-8')}`)

5. KeyPairs

Using a shared key works for encryption works, but the problem is that both parties must agree upon the key. This is problematic in the real world because it’s not practical or secure to share across a network. The

solution is to use an algoritm like RSA that generates a keypair containing a public and private key.

As their names indicate, the private key should be kept secret, while the public key can be shared freely. 
Public Key <---- Mathematically Linked ----> Private Key

Implementation:

const { generateKeyPairSync } = require('crypto') const { privateKey, publicKey } = generateKeyPairSync('rsa', { modulusLength: 2048, // the length of your key in bits publicKeyEncoding: { type: 'spki', // recommended to be 'spki' by the Node.js docs format: 'pem' }, privateKeyEncoding: { type: 'pkcs8', // recommended to be 'pkcs8' by the Node.js docs format: 'pem' }
}) console.log(publicKey)
console.log(privateKey)

6. Asymetric Encryption

Asymmetric encryption depends on two keys. Encrypt a message with the public key and decrypt it with the private key.

Asymmetric encryption is used on the web whenever you use HTTPS to establish an encrypted connection to that website. The browser finds the public key of an SSL certificate installed on the website, which is used to encrypt any data you send, then the private key decrypts it.

Hello World  --encrypt--> {jibbrish} --decrypt--> Hello World
                 |                        |
                 |                        |
              Public Key              Private Key

Implementation:

const { publicEncrypt, privateDecrypt } = require('crypto')
const { publicKey, privateKey } = require('./keypair') const encryptedData = publicEncrypt(publicKey, Buffer.from(secretMessage)) console.log(encryptedData.toString('hex')) const decryptedData = privateDecrypt(privateKey, encryptedData) console.log(decryptedData.toString('utf-8'))

AlexMaldonadoSoto - May 3

Kristen Kinnear-Ohlmann - Apr 18

Items.Title: 探索 SVG 图形第三部分 | Geeking-out on SVG Graphics part-three
Items.Description:
我的项目是为软件工程师创建一个类似于切割垫样式的基于 SVG 的背景（好吧，我，不是其他任何人，为自私而道歉）。

如上一篇文章所述，我有一个 4000 像素 x 2500 像素的网格，其单元格为 250 像素正方形，并以稍厚的矩形为边界。 此外，如上一篇文章所述，我选择了基于工程师标记液 (#191662) 的背景颜色和网格线和副线的灰色 (#777)。

在这第三篇文章中，我将使用实际屏幕尺寸表示少量屏幕分辨率。 我们还将显示比率投影线和标签。 有关更多详细信息，请参阅初始帖子。

我将在每个阶段结束时通过我的GitHub存储库发布切割垫 SVG 图像。

屏幕分辨率

随着计算机显示器技术的进步，我们看到可用于在屏幕上呈现图形信息的像素数量显着增加。 我们还看到显示器变得更宽，但通常屏幕呈现的宽度和高度（以像素为单位）符合行业标准并符合通用比例。

示例包括：

屏幕分辨率宽度高度
最近人们将熟悉平板高清 (HD) 屏幕，其分辨率为 16:9。

屏幕分辨率宽度高度
高清 720p 全高清 1080p 超高清 2K/QHD 1440p 超高清 4K 维基百科有一个关于这个主题的优秀页面。

在项目的这一步中，我想使用实际像素尺寸来表示每个屏幕分辨率。 矩形将锚定到网格的左上角，并带有一个标签，以指示附加到右下角的标准和分辨率。 从原点开始的两条投影线将通过每个矩形的对角线以链接常见的分辨率。

让我们开始编码

我们将分三个阶段更新项目，首先我们建立一个包含三个group元素的包含svg元素。

第一阶段 - SVG 和组容器

将有一组用于比率投影线，第二组用于矩形和标签，第三组用于标题文本和锚点。 该顺序很重要，因为图形将按group元素顺序绘制在彼此的顶部。

 <svg x= "20" y= "20" width= "1360" height= "880" id= "resolution" viewBox= "-125 -125 4250 2750" > <g class= "ratios" ></g> <g class= "rectangles" ></g> <g class= "labels" > <text x= "25" y= "-25" class= "title" > Screen Resolutions </text> <circle cx= "0" cy= "0" r= "15" ></circle> </g> </svg>

上述代码中需要注意的事项包括：

  • 看看svg元素的尺寸 (1360 x 880) 比我想要绘制的最大屏幕分辨率 (3840 x 2160) 小得多。
  • 我们有一个viewBox属性，它将svg元素#resolution中呈现的任何内容向上和向左偏移 125px。 我们还将 1360x880 的实际像素重新映射为 4250x2750 的“虚拟”像素。
  • 第一组和第二组当前为空，但第三组包含标题文本和原点圆的硬编码 SVG 元素。

投影线将从与屏幕分辨率矩形相同的原点开始，并延伸到网格的边缘。 由于网格本身的纵横比为 16:10，两条投影线都不会穿过网格的右下角。 第二阶段 - 更新样式

首先，我们添加一个自定义属性来提供一种通用颜色。

 --resolution-colour : #fcc ;

接下来我们为内容添加样式。

 #resolution line , #resolution rect { stroke : var ( --resolution-colour ); stroke-width : 3 ; fill : transparent ; } #resolution circle { stroke : var ( --resolution-colour ); stroke-width : 2 ; fill : var ( --background-colour ); } #resolution .labels circle , #resolution text { fill : var ( --resolution-colour ); } #resolution .ratios text { font-size : 3rem ; text-anchor : middle ; } #resolution .labels text { font-size : 2.5rem ; text-anchor : end ; } #resolution .labels .title { font-size : 4rem ; text-anchor : start ; }

我们引用SVG元素的id属性来定位它包含的文本、线条、圆形和矩形以应用样式。

最后——我们有了填充空组元素所需的 JavaScript

我们首先更新 main 函数以调用drawResolutions ，该函数为第一组调用drawProjectionLines ，为第二组调用drawScreenResultions ，如下所示。

 ( function () { drawGrid (); drawResolutions (); })(); // : function drawResolutions () { drawProjectionLines (); drawScreenResultions (); }

以下函数遵循类似的模式：

  1. 使用document.querySelector('#resolution ...方法定位 DOM 入口点（组元素）。
  2. 准备要渲染的数据。
  3. 遍历数据并为每个项目生成表示数据所需的 SVG 指令。

绘制投影线

function drawProjectionLines () { const resolutionRatios = document . querySelector ( ' #resolution .ratios ' ); const ratioLines = [ { w : 3333 , h : 2500 , t : ' 4:3 ' }, { w : 4000 , h : 2250 , t : ' 16:9 ' }, ]; ratioLines . forEach ( r => ( resolutionRatios . innerHTML += ` <line x1="0" y1="0" x2=" ${ r . w } " y2=" ${ r . h } " stroke-dasharray="8,8"></line> <circle cx=" ${ r . w } " cy=" ${ r . h } " r="60"></circle> <text x=" ${ r . w } " y=" ${ r . h + 20 } "> ${ r . t } </text> ` ) ); }

因此，我们将组定位在具有ratios类的分辨率SVG元素中。 我们准备了一个数组，其中包含两条投影线的数据，包括宽度 (w)、高度 (h) 和文本 (t) 标签。

对于数组中的每个项目，我们使用模板文字（字符串）为投影线创建 SVG 指令，并使用innerHTML属性将其注入 DOM。 这可能不是最高效的方法，但它可以工作并且性能不是主要问题，尤其是对于两个数据项。

该字符串由三个 SVG 元素组成；

  • 从原点到“w”和“h”坐标的线。 我们将通过stroke-dasharray属性使用虚线作为投影线。
  • 在与网格边缘相交的投影线末端的顶部绘制了一个大的实心圆圈。
  • 在圆圈的边界内，我们添加一个text元素，说明投影线所代表的比率。 文本的垂直位置比圆心低 20px。

画屏结果

所以，我作弊，但有充分的理由。 这两个函数都使用在drawScreenResultions函数中创建并作为属性传递给子函数的相同数据集。 数据由一组对象组成，每个屏幕分辨率一个对象，并包含以下属性：

  • 宽度
  • 高度
  • 我不会在这篇文章中展示数据，因为它很长，但如果你有兴趣，请查看 repo。

    • 在数据已经定义的情况下，子函数只需要在 DOM 中定位入口点并渲染适当的图形。

    入口点是具有rectangles类的组，渲染是从原点开始并使用规定的宽度和高度的矩形，它也用于定位一个小的实心圆。

    使用具有“标签”类的组的 DOM 中的入口点，此函数将文本注入到指定位置。

    我们有它。 我们有一个带有网格、逐行、Creative-commons 链接的切割垫，并展示了（可以说）最常见的屏幕分辨率及其比例投影线的选择。

    在下一部分中，我将添加一些“标准角度”并使用path元素绘制一些弧线，这比声音更难，敬请期待。



    原文:

    My project is to create an SVG-based backdrop in the style of a cutting mat for software engineers (well me, not really anyone else, apologies for being selfish).

    As covered in the previous post, I have a grid 4000px by 2500px with cells of 250px square and bounded with a slightly thicker rectangle. Also, as stated in the previous post, I have chosen a background colour based on engineer's marking fluid (#191662) and a grey (#777) for the grid lines and the by-line.

    In this third post I will be representing a small number of screen resolutions using actual screen dimensions. We will also show ratio projection lines and labels. See the initial post for more details.

    I will release the cutting-mat SVG image at the end of each stage via my GitHub repo.

    Screen Resolutions

    As the technology behind computer displays has progressed we have seen a significant increase in the number of pixels that can be used to present graphical information on screen. We have also seen displays getting wider but in general the width and height a screen presents (in pixels) conforms to an industry standard and align to a common ratio.

    Display units (known as Cathode Ray Tube (CRT) displays) of the 1980's and 90's typically assumed a ratio of 4:3, for every four pixels of width there would be three pixels in height.

    Examples includes:

    Screen Resolutions Width Height
    VGA 640 480
    SVGA 800 600
    XGA 1024 768
    UXGA 1600 1200

    More recently people will be familiar with flat-panel High Definition (HD) screens, which assume a 16:9 resolution ratio.

    Screen Resolutions Width Height
    HD 720p 1280 720
    Full HD 1080p 1920 1080
    UHD 2K/QHD 1440p 2560 1440
    UHD 4K 3840 2160

    Wikipedia has an excellent page on this topic.

    In this step of the project I want to represent each screen resolution using actual pixel dimensions. The rectangles will be anchored to the top-left corner of the grid with a label to indicate the standard and resolution attached to the bottom-right corner. Two projection lines from the origin will pass through the diagonal for each rectangle to link the common resolutions.

    Let's get coding

    We will update the project in three stages, first we establish a containing svg element with three group elements within it.

    Stage One - SVG and group containers

    There will be one group for the ratio projection lines, a second for the rectangles and labels, and a third for the title text and an anchor point. The order is significant as the graphics will be plotted on top of each other in group element order.

     <svg x="20" y="20" width="1360" height="880" id="resolution" viewBox="-125 -125 4250 2750"> <g class="ratios"></g> <g class="rectangles"></g> <g class="labels"> <text x="25" y="-25" class="title">Screen Resolutions</text> <circle cx="0" cy="0" r="15"></circle> </g>
</svg>

    Things to notice in the above code include:

    • See how the dimensions of the svg element (1360 x 880) is considerably smaller than the largest screen resolution I want to plot (3840 x 2160).
    • We have an attribute of viewBox that offsets whatever is presented inside svg element #resolution by 125px up and to the left. We have also remapped the 1360x880 actual pixels to 4250x2750 'virtual' pixels.
    • The first and second groups are currently empty but the third contains hard-coded SVG elements for the title text and the origin circle.

    The projection lines will start at the same origin as the screen resolution rectangles and extend to the edge of the grid. As the grid itself has an aspect ratio of 16:10 neither projection line will pass through the bottom-right corner of the grid. The 16:9 line will pass trough the right-side edge and the 4:3 line will go through the bottom edge.

    Stage two - update the styling

    First we add a custom property to provide a common colour.

     --resolution-colour: #fcc;

    Next we add styling for the content.

    #resolution line,
#resolution rect { stroke: var(--resolution-colour); stroke-width: 3; fill: transparent;
} #resolution circle { stroke: var(--resolution-colour); stroke-width: 2; fill: var(--background-colour);
} #resolution .labels circle,
#resolution text { fill: var(--resolution-colour);
} #resolution .ratios text { font-size: 3rem; text-anchor: middle; } #resolution .labels text { font-size: 2.5rem; text-anchor: end;
} #resolution .labels .title { font-size: 4rem; text-anchor: start; }

    We are referencing the id attribute of the SVG element to target the text, lines, circles and rectangles it contains to apply styling.

    Finally - We have the JavaScript required to populate the empty group elements

    We start by updating the main function to call drawResolutions that calls drawProjectionLines for the first group and drawScreenResultions for the second group, as follows.

    (function () { drawGrid(); drawResolutions();
})(); // : function drawResolutions() { drawProjectionLines(); drawScreenResultions();
}

    The following functions follow a similar pattern:

    1. Locate the DOM entry point (group element) using the document.querySelector('#resolution ... method.
    2. Prepare the data to be rendered.
    3. Traverse the data and for each item generate the SVG instruction(s) required to represent the data.

    drawProjectionLines 

    function drawProjectionLines() { const resolutionRatios = document.querySelector('#resolution .ratios'); const ratioLines = [ { w: 3333, h: 2500, t: '4:3' }, { w: 4000, h: 2250, t: '16:9' }, ]; ratioLines.forEach( r => (resolutionRatios.innerHTML += ` <line x1="0" y1="0" x2="${r.w}" y2="${r.h}" stroke-dasharray="8,8"></line> <circle cx="${r.w}" cy="${r.h}" r="60"></circle> <text x="${r.w}" y="${r.h + 20}">${r.t}</text> `) );
}

    So, we locate the group in the resolution SVG element with a class of ratios. We prepare an array contain the data for the two projection lines including the width (w), height (h) and text (t) label.

    For each item in the array we create the SVG instructions for a projection line using a template literal (string) and inject it into the DOM using the innerHTML attribute. This might not be the most performant approach but it works and performance is not a major concern, especially for two data items.

    The string consists of three SVG elements;

    • the line running from the origin to the 'w' and 'h' coordinates. We will use a dashed line for the projection lines via the stroke-dasharray attribute.
    • a large filled circle is drawn on top of the end of the projection line where is intersects with the edge of the grid.
    • within the boundary of the circle we add a text element stating the ratio the projection line represents. The vertical location of the text is 20px lower than the centre of the circle.

    drawScreenResultions

    So, I cheated but for good reason. The drawScreenResultions function is actually split in two further function calls:

    • The drawScreenResultionsRectangles function draws the rectangles representing the screen resolutions.
    • The drawScreenResultionsLabels function adds the labels, which uses a different resolution otherwise the text would be too tinu to read. Both functions use the same data set that is created in the drawScreenResultions function and passed as an attribute to the sub functions. The data consists of an array of objects, one for each screen resolution, and containing the following properties:
    • width
    • height
    • spec: the name of the standard the resolution applies
    • xOffset: the number of pixels left/right to shift the text so it aligns with the rectangle.
    • yOffset: similar to xOffset, it is used to position the text relative to the rectangle representing the screen resolution. I will not present the data in this post as it is quite lengthy but ask you check out the repo if you are interested.

    With the data already defined the sub functions just have to locate the entry point in the DOM and render the appropriate graphics.

    drawScreenResultionsRectangles

    The entry point is the group with the class of rectangles and the rendering is rectangle that starts at the origin and uses the prescribed width and height, which is also used to locate a small filled circle.

    drawScreenResultionsLabels

    Using the entry point in the DOM of the group with the 'labels' class, this function injects the text at the prescribed location.

    There we have it. We have a cutting-mat with a grid, by-line, Creative-commons link and presenting a selection of the (arguably) most common screen resolutions along with their ratio projection lines.

    In the next instalment I will be adding some 'standard angles' and using the path element to draw some arcs, which is harder than is sounds so stay tuned.
Items.Author: tracygjg

Items.Title: 如何使用库项目改进编码时间 | How to Improve Coding Time Using Library Project
Items.Description:
库不过是一组文件，会在其他项目中使用，不能直接运行。 我们可以将 Facebook SDK 或 Google SDK 视为一个库。

为什么我们需要图书馆？
这个问题必须在每个人的脑海中浮现，因为没有人愿意浪费他/她的时间来开发一个永远没有用的东西。 这真的是浪费时间还是对未来的投资？

让我举一个我目前看到的真实例子。 我们为一位客户开发了一个应用程序（例如聊天应用程序）。 申请成功，客户对我们的工作很满意。 现在客户需要另一个聊天应用程序，只改变颜色，或者我们可以说另一个应用程序需要一个聊天功能。

现在我们有两个选择。

选项 1 ：我们可以为这两个应用程序制作标准源代码（它作为一个库工作）

选项 2 ：我们可以将聊天功能复制并粘贴到另一个应用程序中。

我们可以对这个应用程序使用任何方法，因为此时它对效率没有任何影响。

现在让我们看看未来； 在第二个应用程序成功后，客户想要第三个应用程序进行较小的更新。 我们可以再次克隆应用程序并执行相同的操作。 每次克隆和更新都耗费时间并影响专家的效率。 但是让我们看看通过使用库可以灵活到什么程度。

图书馆的好处
可重用性- 克隆应用程序不是一个好的解决方案，因为它不可重用。 一旦您创建了用于聊天的通用模块并将其发布为库，任何人和每个人都可以插入该库并使用它，而不会影响其他代码或与包相关的问题。 它可以根据我们的需要多次重复使用。

节省时间- 是否节省时间？ 我们需要为所有应用程序创建一个标准模块。 因此，如果有任何变化，我们只需要担心一个地方。 如果我们添加新功能，所有连接的应用程序都会自动更新。

易于维护- 维护一个代码库比维护多个应用程序简单快捷。 它易于维护，因为我们可以从一个地方更新或修改代码。

易于调试和测试- 一个库使您的调试和测试变得容易。 我们只有一个代码库，所以我们只需要测试一次。

更快的构建时间- 例如，客户想要开发一个我们分成 4 个部分模块的应用程序。 另一方面，我们已经有一个包含 3 个所需模块的库。 因此，我们只需要处理 1 个模块，因为其他模块已准备好与我们一起作为库使用。 这样，我们可以加快应用程序的构建时间。

图书馆如何运作？
Library Module > App Module > App

我们可以在同一个项目中创建一个库作为另一个模块，或者单独发布我们的库并将其导入到我们的项目中。 如果库需要它们，我们需要传递一些参数，我们准备好了。 听起来很简单吧？ 但这并不像听起来那么简单。 建立图书馆需要时间。

为 Android 创建库模块的步骤

这就像创建一个新项目。 可以从 File > New > New Module 创建一个新模块。

查看此图像，它将帮助您创建一个新模块作为库模块。

现在我们要做的是在库模块中编写代码。 只是为了演示，创建“Helper.kt”目标文件。 编写一个记录详细信息的函数：

fun writeLog(logText: String) { var modifiedLogText = "$currentTime - $logText" if(debugBuild) { Log.d("AppLog", modifiedLogText); } else { writeLogInFile(modifiedLogText); } }
现在是时候在项目中使用它了，将模块依赖项添加到当前项目模块中，我们已经准备好使用我们的库，它将以我们的格式记录文本。 这个模块很容易复制粘贴到任何项目中，或者我们也可以发布这个库并像材料库一样使用它。

单行代码示例，一旦它在 GitHub 等一些常见平台上可用，我们就可以使用它来导入库。

  • 实施 'com.google.android.material:material:1.5.0'

  • 实施 'com.myownlibrary:1.0.0'

结论

最好工作一次，每次都使用结果，而不是每次都克隆。 我们可以通过创建库模块或创建 utils 文件来节省开发时间。 切勿在同一项目或类似项目中复制和粘贴。 始终寻找可以放置该代码并重复使用它的空间。

从 The One Technologies雇用 android 开发人员。 他们用勤奋的工作做聪明的工作，并在更短的时间内提供出色的输出。



原文:

A library is nothing but a set of files, which will be used in other projects, and it can not be directly runnable. We can consider Facebook SDK or Google SDK as a library.

Why do we need a library?
This question must arise in everyone's mind because no one wants to waste his/her time developing a thing that is never useful. Is it really a waste of time or an investment for the future?

Let me give you a real example that I have currently seen. There is a client for whom we have developed one application (ex. Chat app). The application is successful, and the client is happy with our work. Now client needs another chat app with only a color change, or we can say that another app needs a chat function within.

Now we have two options.

Option 1: We can make standard source code for both apps (It works as a library)

Option 2: we can just copy and paste chat functionality into another app.

We can use any approach for this app because, at this time, it doesn't have any effect on efficiency.

Now let's see the future; after the success of the second app, the client wants the third app with minor updates. We can again clone the app and does the same thing. Every clone and update is consuming time and impacts the efficiency of experts. But let's see how much it can be flexible through the use of a library.

Benefits of library
Reusability - Clone the app is not a good solution because it is not reusable. Once you have created common module for chatting and published it as a library, anyone and everyone can plug the library in and use it without affecting other code or package-related issues. It can be reusable as many times as we want.

Time-Saving - Is it time-saving or not? We need to create one standard module for all apps. So if there are any changes, we need to worry about just one place. If we add new features, all connected apps will be updated automatically.

Easy Maintenance - Maintaining one codebase is easy and fast than maintaining multiple apps. It is easy to maintain as we can update or modify the code from one place.

Easy debugging and Testing - A library makes your debugging and testing easy. We have only one codebase, so we need to test only once.

Faster build time - For example, the client wants to develop an app that we divided into 4 part modules. On-the-other hand, we already had a library for 3 desired modules. So, we need to work on only 1 module as others are ready to use with us as a library. This way, we can quicken the app build time.

How does the Library Work?
Library Module > App Module > App

We can create a library as another module in the same project or publish our library separately and import it into our project. We need to pass a few parameters if the library requires them, and we are ready to rock. It sounds simple right? But it is not as simple as it sounds. It takes time to set up a library.

"SharedPreference” wrapper in Android or "DateTimeFormatter" library is commonly helpful in almost every app.

Step to Create Library Module for Android

It is like creating a new project. One can create a new module from File > New > New Module.

Check out this image that will help you create a new module as a library module.

Now what we have to do is write code inside the library module. Just for the demo, create “Helper.kt” object file. write a function that will log details:

fun writeLog(logText: String) { var modifiedLogText = "$currentTime - $logText" if(debugBuild) { Log.d("AppLog", modifiedLogText); } else { writeLogInFile(modifiedLogText); } }
Now it is time to use it in a project, add module dependency to the current project module, and we are ready with our library, which will log text in our format. This module is easy to copy-paste into any project, or we can also publish this library and use it just like a material library.

Example of single-line code that we can use to import the library once it is available on some common platforms like GitHub.

  • Implementation 'com.google.android.material:material:1.5.0'

  • implementation 'com.myownlibrary:1.0.0'

Conclusion

It is better to work once and use the outcome each time required, rather than cloning every time. We can save time for development by creating a library module or creating a utils file. Never copy and paste inside the same project or similar project. Always look for space where you can just put that code and use it repeatedly.

Hire android developers from The One Technologies. They do smart work with hard work and deliver excellent output in less time.

Items.Title: YMD 下拉日期选择器的 React 库 | A React library for Y-M-D dropdown date picker
Items.Description:
我创建了react-ymd-date-select ，一个用于年、月和日下拉 UI 的 React 库。

我们有时需要像上面截图那样的 YMD 下拉 UI，而不是日历 UI。 例如，当要求用户选择他们的出生日期时，YMD 下拉菜单更可取，因为日历小部件通常很难找到很久以前的年份，相反，它不需要直观地显示星期几等好处。

我实际上遇到过这种情况，但是，我找不到任何满足我需求的现有软件包。 反过来，我自己构建它看起来很简单，但实际上，有一些坚果如下。

  • 生成年、月、日数字和标签的数组。
  • 验证 YMD 的组合。 例如， 2022-02-29 （2022 年 2 月 29 日）是无效的组合 - 该日期不存在。
  • 将 Y、M、D <select > s 中的 3 个值组合成一个日期字符串，并通过value和onChange props 将其与表单组件状态或表单库集成，例如React Hook Form 。

有时会有更多的要求，比如

  • 设置默认年份。
  • 仅显示年份和月份选择（隐藏日期下拉菜单）。

所以，为了处理这些，我制作了一个库react-ymd-date-select 。

使用该库提供的useDateSelect()钩子，您可以像下面的示例一样创建原始的 YMD 组件，专注于视图行为，因为繁琐的逻辑已被封装到钩子中。

请参阅https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/input/date#value

有关更多信息，请参阅演示页面。

 import { useState } from "react"; import { useDateSelect } from "react-ymd-date-select"; interface CustomDateSelectProps { onChange: (value: string) => void; value: string; } function CustomDateSelect(props: CustomDateSelectProps) { const dateSelect = useDateSelect(props.value, props.onChange); return ( <> <input type="date" value={dateSelect.dateValue || ""} onChange={dateSelect.onDateChange} /> <label> Year <select value={dateSelect.yearValue} onChange={dateSelect.onYearChange}> {dateSelect.yearOptions.map((yearOption) => ( <option key={yearOption.value} value={yearOption.value}> {yearOption.label} </option> ))} </select> </label> <label> Month <select value={dateSelect.monthValue} onChange={dateSelect.onMonthChange} > {dateSelect.monthOptions.map((monthOption) => ( <option key={monthOption.value} value={monthOption.value}> {monthOption.label} </option> ))} </select> </label> <label> Day <select value={dateSelect.dayValue} onChange={dateSelect.onDayChange}> {dateSelect.dayOptions.map((dayOption) => ( <option key={dayOption.value} value={dayOption.value}> {dayOption.label} </option> ))} </select> </label> </> ); }


原文:

I created react-ymd-date-select, a React library for Year, Month, and Day dropdown UI.

whitphx/react-ymd-date-select - GitHub

We sometimes need Y-M-D dropdown UI like the screenshot above rather than calendar UI. For example, when asking users to select their birth dates, Y-M-D dropdown is preferable because calendar widgets are usually difficult to seek to far old years, and conversely, its benefits such as intuitively showing days-of-week are not needed.

I actually encountered such a situation, however, I could not find any existing packages that meet my needs. In turn, building it by myself looks simple, but actually, there are some nuts as below.

  • Generating the arrays of year, month, and day numbers and labels.
  • Validating the combination of Y-M-D. For example, 2022-02-29 (Feb 29, 2022) is an invalid combination - that date does not exist.
  • Combining the 3 values from Y, M, D <select >s into one date string, and integrating it with the form component state or form library, e.g. React Hook Form through value and onChange props.

And sometimes there come more requirements like

  • To set the default year.
  • To show only year and month selects (hide the day dropdown).

So, to handle these, I made a library react-ymd-date-select.

With the useDateSelect() hook provided by this library, you can create original Y-M-D components like the sample below, focusing on the view behavior as cumbersome logic has been encapsulated into the hook.

The value prop and the argument of onChange() are string formatted in yyyy-MM-DD, which is ISO8601 format, and the same as the value of the native <input type="date" />. See https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/input/date#value

See the demo page for more information.

import { useState } from "react";
import { useDateSelect } from "react-ymd-date-select";

interface CustomDateSelectProps {
  onChange: (value: string) => void;
  value: string;
}
function CustomDateSelect(props: CustomDateSelectProps) {
  const dateSelect = useDateSelect(props.value, props.onChange);

  return (
    <>
      <input
        type="date"
        value={dateSelect.dateValue || ""}
        onChange={dateSelect.onDateChange}
      />
      <label>
        Year
        <select value={dateSelect.yearValue} onChange={dateSelect.onYearChange}>
          {dateSelect.yearOptions.map((yearOption) => (
            <option key={yearOption.value} value={yearOption.value}>
              {yearOption.label}
            </option>
          ))}
        </select>
      </label>
      <label>
        Month
        <select
          value={dateSelect.monthValue}
          onChange={dateSelect.onMonthChange}
        >
          {dateSelect.monthOptions.map((monthOption) => (
            <option key={monthOption.value} value={monthOption.value}>
              {monthOption.label}
            </option>
          ))}
        </select>
      </label>
      <label>
        Day
        <select value={dateSelect.dayValue} onChange={dateSelect.onDayChange}>
          {dateSelect.dayOptions.map((dayOption) => (
            <option key={dayOption.value} value={dayOption.value}>
              {dayOption.label}
            </option>
          ))}
        </select>
      </label>
    </>
  );
}
Items.Author: whitphx

Items.Title: 在Javascript中使用递归合并两个数组并对结果进行排序而不使用排序函数 | Merging two arrays and sort the resultant without sort functions using recursion in Javascript
Items.Description:
尼廷·西瓦利

发表于很久以前，在一家产品型公司的面试中，面试官问了我一个 DSA 问题，这引发了我的第六感。

所以问题是合并两个数组并按升序对结果数组进行排序。 例子：- 

 const arr1 = [2,4,8,7, 10, 25, 17, 13, 12 ]; const arr2 = [1,4,6,9,8, 11, 13, 3, 0]; So I used spread operator to merge two arrays and Array.sort() function to sort the values. const resultant = [...arr1, ...arr2].sort((a, b) => ab);

这将给出预期的结果。

然后面试官让我不要使用排序功能并自己创建一个，解释说它需要两个循环。 所以我不得不向他展示 javascript 的强大功能，我使用递归而不是两个循环。

 const result = [...arr1, ...arr2]; let count = 0; function sorting(){ // to break the recursion when sorting is done. if(count ===result.length) return true; //else do for(let i = 0; i< result.length; i++){ if(result[i] > result[i+1]){ //used array destructing to swap values [ result[i+1], result[i]]= [result[i],result[i+1]]; } } count++; sorting(); } sorting();

在 javascript 中，递归是最被低估的概念。 当 DSA 与 javascript 一起使用时，这变得非常强大。 它可以展平数组、对象，并构建像 UI 一样的树结构，等等。

伙计们，这就是现在的全部内容。 继续学习并相信 Javascript。



原文:

Nitin Sijwali

Posted on May 12

Long ago, during an interview for one of the product-based companies, the interviewer asked me a DSA question, which triggered my sixth sense.

So the question was to merge two arrays and sort the resultant array in ascending order. Example:-

const arr1 = [2,4,8,7, 10, 25, 17, 13, 12 ];
const arr2 = [1,4,6,9,8, 11, 13, 3, 0];
So I used spread operator to merge two arrays and  Array.sort() function to sort the values.
const resultant = [...arr1, ...arr2].sort((a, b) => a-b);

This will give the desired result.

The interviewer then asked me not to use the sort function and to create one on my own, explaining that it would require two loops. So I had to demonstrate the power of javascript to him, and I used recursion instead of two loops.

const result = [...arr1, ...arr2];
let count = 0;
function sorting(){
// to break the recursion when sorting is done.
if(count ===result.length) return true; 

//else do
for(let i = 0; i< result.length; i++){
  if(result[i] > result[i+1]){

//used array destructing to swap values
   [ result[i+1],  result[i]]= [result[i],result[i+1]]; 
  }
}
count++;
sorting();
}
sorting();

In javascript, recursion is the most underappreciated concept. When DSA is used with javascript, this becomes quite strong. It can flatten arrays, objects, and construct tree structures like UI, among other things.

That's all for now, folks. Continue to learn and have faith in Javascript.

Items.Title: AWS 上存储服务的弹性 | Resilience of Storage Services on AWS
Items.Description:
在 AWS 上构建解决方案时，了解 AWS 云提供的数据弹性非常重要。

在这篇文章中，我将讨论 AWS 上存储服务的弹性。 我们将研究以下 AWS 存储服务：

  • 实例存储
  • 亚马逊弹性块存储 (Amazon EBS)
  • 适用于 Windows 的 Amazon FSx
  • 用于 Lustre 的 Amazon FSx
  • 亚马逊简单存储服务 (Amazon S3)
  • 实例存储

    实例存储为 EC2 实例提供临时块级存储。

    实例存储不提供任何级别的弹性。

    实例存储中的数据仅在其关联实例的生命周期内持续存在。 如果实例停止、休眠、终止或底层磁盘驱动器出现故障，实例存储中的数据就会丢失。 但是，实例存储中的数据会在实例重新启动时保留。

    亚马逊弹性块存储 (Amazon EBS)

    使用 EFS 标准存储类，每个 EFS 文件系统对象（例如目录、文件和链接）都以冗余方式存储在多个可用区中。 您可以将应用程序架构为从一个 AZ 故障转移到区域中的其他 AZ，以确保最高级别的应用程序可用性。

    借助 EFS One Zone 存储类，您的数据以冗余方式存储在单个可用区内。

    挂载目标设计为在 AZ 内对所有 EFS 存储类都具有高可用性。

    适用于 Windows 的 Amazon FSx

    单 AZ 文件系统旨在通过在单个 AZ 内复制数据在可用区内具有弹性。 多可用区文件系统提供跨多个可用区的冗余。

    如果活动文件服务器或 AZ 发生故障，Amazon FSx 会自动故障转移到备用服务器，这样您就可以恢复文件系统操作，而不会丢失数据的可用性。

    用于 Lustre 的 Amazon FSx

    使用持久文件系统在可用区内复制的数据。

    持久性文件系统是长期存储和以吞吐量为重点的工作负载的理想选择。 在持久文件系统中，数据在 AZ 内复制，如果文件服务器出现故障，则会自动替换它们。

    暂存文件系统是临时存储和短期数据处理的理想选择。 如果文件服务器出现故障，数据不会被复制并且不会持续存在。

    亚马逊简单存储服务 (Amazon S3)

    通过这种集成，Storage Gateway 可在 60 秒内自动从大多数服务中断中恢复（无论是部署在本地 VMware 环境中，还是部署在 VMware Cloud on AWS 中）。 这可以保护存储工作负载免受硬件、管理程序或网络故障、存储错误以及导致连接超时或文件共享、卷或磁带不可用的软件问题的影响。

    结论
    我希望这篇文章可以帮助您了解如何使用各种 AWS 存储服务提供的内置弹性将您的数据可靠地存储在 AWS 上。



原文:

AWS has the most reliable Global Infrastructure for cloud that allows you to build secure, scalable and highly available workloads in the cloud. While building solutions on AWS, it is important to know the data resiliency offered by AWS cloud.

In this post I will talk about the resiliency of storage services on AWS. We will look at the following AWS storage services:

  • Instance Store
  • Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS)
  • Amazon Elastic File System (Amazon EFS)
  • Amazon FSx for Windows
  • Amazon FSx for Lustre
  • Amazon Simple Storage Service (Amazon S3)
  • AWS Storage Gateway

Instance Store

Instance stores provides temporary block-level storage for EC2 instances.

Instance Stores do not offer any level of resiliency.

The data in an instance store persists only during the lifetime of its associated instance. The data in the instance store is lost if the instance stops, hibernates, terminates or the underlying disk drive fails. However, data in the instance store is persisted in case of instance reboot.

Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS)

Amazon EBS provides block-level persistent storage volumes attached to EC2 instances.

EBS volumes are replicated within an Availability Zone (AZ).

Amazon EBS volumes are designed to be highly available, reliable, and durable. Amazon EBS volume data is replicated across multiple servers in an Availability Zone to prevent the loss of data from the failure of any single component.

Amazon EBS also has snapshot feature to help support your data resiliency and backup needs.

Amazon Elastic File System (Amazon EFS)

Amazon EFS provides a simple, serverless, set-and-forget elastic file system.

Amazon EFS file systems (except for EFS One Zone) are resilient to one or more Availability Zone failures within an AWS Region.

With EFS Standard storage classes, every EFS file system object (such as directory, file, and link) is redundantly stored across multiple Availability Zones. You can architect your application to failover from one AZ to other AZs in the Region to ensure the highest level of application availability.

With EFS One Zone storage classes, your data is redundantly stored within a single Availability Zone.

Mount targets are designed to be highly available within an AZ for all EFS storage classes.

Amazon EFS is designed to sustain concurrent device failures by quickly detecting and repairing any lost redundancy.

Amazon EFS also supports resiliency needs using features such as EFS Replication and data backup using AWS Backup or EFS-to-EFS backup.

Amazon FSx for Windows

Amazon FSx for Windows provides fully managed file storage built on Windows Server.

Single-AZ file systems designed to be resilient within an Availability Zone by replicating data within single AZ. Multi-AZ file systems provide redundancy across multiple AZs.

Amazon FSx automatically replicates your data within an Availability Zone to protect it from component failure and automatically replaces infrastructure components in the event of a failure.

Amazon FSx offers Multi-AZ option that include an active and standby file server in separate AZs. In the event of a failure of the active file server or an AZ, Amazon FSx automatically fails over to the standby so you can resume file system operations without a loss of availability to your data.

Amazon FSx also automatically takes highly durable, file-system consistent daily backups to S3 (using Volume Shadow Copy Service), and allows you to take additional backups at any point.

Amazon FSx for Lustre

Amazon FSx for Lustre provides fully managed shared storage with the scalability and performance of the popular Lustre file system.

Data replicated within an Availability Zone with Persistent file system.

Amazon FSx for Lustre provides a parallel file system, where data is stored across multiple network file servers to maximize performance and reduce bottlenecks.

Amazon FSx for Lustre offers a choice of scratch and persistent file systems to accommodate different data processing needs.

Persistent file systems are ideal for longer-term storage and throughput-focused workloads. In persistent file systems, data is replicated within an AZ, and file servers are replaced automatically if they fail.

Scratch file systems are ideal for temporary storage and shorter-term processing of data. Data is not replicated and does not persist if a file server fails.

Amazon Simple Storage Service (Amazon S3)

Amazon S3 is an object storage service built to store and retrieve any amount of data from anywhere.

S3 objects (except for S3 One Zone-IA) are resilient to three or more Availability Zones in an AWS Region.

S3 Standard, S3 Standard-IA, S3 Intelligent-Tiering, S3 Glacier Instant Retrieval, S3 Glacier Flexible Retrieval, and S3 Glacier Deep Archive storage - these storage classes redundantly store objects on multiple devices spanning a minimum of three Availability Zones in an AWS Region.

The S3 One Zone-IA storage class stores data redundantly across multiple devices within a single Availability Zone.

Amazon S3 service is designed to sustain concurrent device failures by quickly detecting and repairing any lost redundancy, and it also regularly verifies the integrity of your data using checksums.

Amazon S3 offers several other features to help support your data resiliency and backup needs such as lifecycle configuration, versioning, object locking and replication.

AWS Storage Gateway

AWS Storage Gateway is a hybrid cloud storage service that provide on-premises applications with access to virtually unlimited cloud storage.

AWS Storage Gateway provides High Availability on VMware.

Storage Gateway achieves high availability on VMware through a series of continuous health-checks against critical operations of the gateway that is integrated with VMware vSphere High Availability (VMware HA). With this integration, Storage Gateway automatically recovers from most service interruptions in under 60 seconds (whether deployed in an on-premises VMware environment, or in VMware Cloud on AWS). This protects storage workloads against hardware, hypervisor, or network failures, storage errors, as well as software issues that lead to connection timeouts or file-share, volume, or tape unavailability.

Storage Gateway also supports your data resiliency and backup needs using features such as volume backup, volume cloning, and tape archival.

Conclusion
I hope this post helps you understand how your data is reliably stored on AWS using the built-in resiliency offered by various AWS Storage Services.

Items.Title: 选择排序 | Selection Sort
Items.Description:
我们对选择排序了解多少？

  • 一旦我们理解了这个概念，就很简单了。
  • 这种排序算法与冒泡排序非常相似
  • 它改变原始数组而不是输出一个新数组
  • 两者都使用 2 个 for 循环，并且执行相同的标准交换逻辑
  • 它的外循环保存着“最小”比较值的索引指针
  • 它的内部循环搜索比当前“最小”指针值更小的值
  • 一旦做出最小的选择。
  • 然后在外循环中进行交换。
  • 在外循环中以增量方式重复相同的操作。
  • 时间复杂度：
    • 最佳：O(n^2)
    • 平均：O(n^2)
    • 最差：O(n^2)
  • 空间复杂度：
 function SelectionSort ( arr ) { const arrayLength = arr . length ; for ( let i = 0 ; i < arrayLength - 1 ; i ++ ) { let smallestIndexPointer = i ; for ( let j = i + 1 ; j < arrayLength ; j ++ ) { if ( arr [ j ] < arr [ smallestIndexPointer ] > ) { smallestIndexPointer = j ; } } if ( smallestIndexPointer !== i ) { [ arr [ i ], arr [ smallestIndexPointer ]] = [ arr [ smallestIndexPointer ], arr [ i ]]; } } return arr ; }

拉姆·马赫什瓦里 ⚡ - May 11 5月11日

Jan Schenk (he/him) - Jan Schenk (他/他) - May 9 5月9日



原文:

What do we understand about Selection Sort?

  • Pretty simple once we understand the concept.
  • This sorting algorithm is quite similar to Bubble Sort
  • It mutates the original Array rather than output a new Array
  • Both use 2 for-loops and also does the same standard swapping logic
  • Its outer loop holds the index pointer of the "smallest" comparison value
  • Its inner loop searches for a smaller value than the current "smallest" pointer value
  • ONCE the smallest selection is made.
  • THEN do the swap in the outer loop.
  • REPEAT the same incrementally in the outer loop.
  • Time Complexity:
    • Best : O(n^2)
    • Average: O(n^2)
    • Worst : O(n^2)
  • Space Complexity: 
 function SelectionSort(arr) { const arrayLength = arr.length; for (let i = 0; i < arrayLength - 1; i++) { let smallestIndexPointer = i; for (let j = i + 1; j < arrayLength; j++) { if (arr[j] < arr[smallestIndexPointer]>) { smallestIndexPointer = j; } } if (smallestIndexPointer !== i) { [arr[i], arr[smallestIndexPointer]] = [arr[smallestIndexPointer], arr[i]]; } } return arr; }

Ram Maheshwari ⚡ - May 11

Jan Schenk (he/him) - May 9
Items.Author: henryong92

Items.Title: Javascript 中的数组 | Arrays in Javascript
Items.Description:
让我们看看如何在 javascript 中执行以下操作 -

  • 如何将values的collections存储在数组中

  • 如何loop array以将重复代码应用于collection中的每个值

  • 如何创建和调用functions以按需执行后续代码块

  • 如何pass values into functions以创建动态结果

我们一直在变量中存储一条数据

var name = "Arya"; var books = 4; var isStudent = true;

数组用于将数据组存储在单个变量中。

 var names = ["Arya", "Krishna", "Manvi", "Amogh"];

我们命名数组的方式与命名单个变量的方式相同。 我们命名一个变量，使用赋值运算符并为数组列表赋值。 我们将值分配给数组的关键语法或方式是，我们键入一组[]作为值，并用逗号分隔值，我们将每个元素写入数组列表中。

在上面的 var 名称示例中，我们在方括号内创建了一个包含 4 个字符串值的序列集合。

请记住，数组是一个有序列表，这意味着所有元素都被分配了一个位置。

数组是零索引的，所以数组中第一个元素的索引是 0。
这意味着当我们试图在数组中分配一个位置时，我们从零开始计数。

如果我们必须去访问一个特定的值，那么我们将继续使用变量名并用方括号扩展它，并使用索引指针指向数组中的特定项。 这意味着要记录单个元素，我们使用数组的名称和括号中的索引



原文:

Let us see how we can do the following in javascript -

  • How to store collections of values in arrays

  • How to loop over anarray to apply repetitive code to each value in a collection

  • How to create and call functions to execute sequel blocks of code on demand

  • How to pass values into functions to create dynamic results

We have been storing one piece of data in variables

var name = "Arya";
var books = 4; 
var isStudent = true;

Arrays are used to store groups of data in a single variable.

var names = ["Arya", "Krishna", "Manvi", "Amogh"];

We name arrays in the same way as we did for a single variable. We name a variable, use the assignment operator and assign a value to the array list. The key syntax or the way we assign the value to an array is that we type a set of []as a value and with coma separating values we write each elements in an array list.

In the above example of var names, we have created a sequence collection of 4 string values inside square brackets.

Remember that arrays are an ordered list means all the elements are assigned a position.

Arrays are zero-indexed, so the index of first element in the array is 0.
That means when we are trying to assign a position in an array, we count up from zero.

If we have to go and access a specific value, then we will go ahead and use the variable name again and extending it with square brackets and using the index pointer to point towards a specific item in the array. That means to log a single element, we use the name of the array with the index in brackets
