Items.Title: 关于 DevOps：1. 它是什么 | On DevOps: 1. What It Is
Items.Description:
注意：意见是我自己的。 他们不代表我现在的公司或我以前工作过的任何公司。

注2：最近，我的同事发表了一篇博文（中文，如果你有兴趣， 这里是链接）。 我喜欢这篇文章，但我想与你分享更多关于 DevOps 的话题。 所以我决定出版一部关于 DevOps 的迷你剧（计划 7 集）。 这是第一篇文章。

注 3：本文最初发表于 2021 年 1 月 14 日，在我的 DevOps Chat 出版物中的 Medium 上，名为 4th Coffee 。 如果您有兴趣，请看这里。 本帖于 2022 年 5 月 10 日再次编辑。

自 2016 年以来，我一直担任 DevOps 工程师，到 2022 年的今天，我仍在从事这项工作（作为DevStream的 PMC）。 但本文不代表 AWS 的观点； 也不代表我目前的公司观点。 这是我个人的观点，是我在多年的项目实践经验中学习和形成的。

所以我想我应该抓住这个机会，把我的想法变成文字，并与社区分享。

在我的职业生涯中，我曾在不同的行业工作过，包括大学研究、搜索引擎、政府、互联网、企业软件、移动、营销商业智能、物流、汽车、制药、保险、咨询等； 我曾在不同类型的公司和项目中工作过：国有企业、本土互联网巨头、全球IT企业、外国初创企业、全球传统组织等。不得不说，我学到了很多，成长了很多在这个伟大的旅程中，无论是作为一个人还是作为一名工程师（全栈开发人员和 DevOps 工程师）。 我还参加了一些会议和聚会，甚至在其中发表了一些演讲，但我从来没有机会总结我的工作并与更多的观众分享知识，这就是我想开始这个系列的原因。

什么是 DevOps？

让我先给你讲个故事。

在过去的 3 年里，我采访了 200 多名工程师，有时我想问这个问题。 这可能毫无意义； 我只是问它，因为我想看看它对每个人意味着什么。

在我得到的所有答案中，似乎大多数人总是以这句话开头：“这是一个好问题。” 然后这个人开始解释他的想法。

我不是以英语为母语的人，但多年来，我逐渐了解到“这是个好问题”等于“我不知道”的事实。

这是一个无意义的填充词，用来填补谈话中尴尬的沉默，它可以让你在回答问题之前有一些时间来整理你的想法。

只有在极少数情况下，您才会听到这句话，因为您确实提出了一个很好的问题。

在过去的 5 年中，我还（作为受访者）采访了许多公司，包括初创企业和大公司。 我也经常被问到这个问题，至少比我预期的要多。 有时，在谈话中，你开始意识到提出这个问题的人或公司也并不真正知道答案； 他们问这个是因为他们想向你学习，看看你能给团队带来什么新想法。

为什么定义 DevOps 这么难？

为什么这个问题很难回答？

因为，在我看来，DevOps 很多。

让我们试着弄清楚

在讨论 DevOps 之前，让我先引用一些定义。

这里我引用三个定义，它们来自 Wikipedia（因为它就像一个互联网词典）、AWS（因为我们都听说 AWS 做 DevOps 很好）和 Atlassian（因为作为 DevOps，我们可能经常使用他们的工具，所以他们可能知道一两件事）：

维基百科：

它旨在缩短系统开发生命周期并提供具有高软件质量的持续交付。 这种速度使组织能够更好地为客户服务并在市场上更有效地竞争。

阿特拉斯：

似乎有一些相似之处，它们是：

当然也有区别，让我们一一来看：

开发和运维的结合

维基百科说这是 Dev 和 Ops 的结合。

这基本上没有说明什么，因为即使我对这个主题一无所知，我已经从这个词本身知道它结合了 Dev 和 Ops。

一个很好的问题是：DevOps 真的是 Dev + Ops 吗？

是的，不。

是的，因为您需要 Dev 和 Ops 的技能组合。

不，因为简单地将两个团队放在一起不会让你到达那里。

文化哲学

假设您拥有 Dev 和 Ops 的工具和技能集。 这足以使您获得更高的速度吗？

可能不是。

事实上，您的组织中已经拥有 Dev 和 Ops 团队以及相应的技能组合。 然后你选择了一个在 DevOps 世界中很有名的新 CI 工具。 你能更快地交付吗？

我不这么认为。

这是因为我们如何做事比我们拥有的更重要，而且根据定义，“我们如何做事”是文化的一部分。

文化

文化是人类社会的行为方式。 风俗是什么，规范是什么。

例如：

  • 什么是对的，什么是错的？
  • 什么可以接受，什么不可以？
  • 你是怎么学习的？
  • 更重要的是，你如何鼓励团队学习？

文化可以是高度技术性的。 例如：您如何进行代码审查？ 哪些类型的代码是可接受的，哪些不是？

它也可能是高度非技术性的。 例如：我们在招聘和晋升时使用什么标准？

那么，DevOps 文化到底是什么？

您可能经常看到这张图片（或与此非常相似的图片）。 老实说，DevOps 文化隐藏在这张图片中：

您不会将 Dev 和 Ops 分开。 你们有共同的理解和共同的责任。 您通过协作和沟通承担整体责任。 这是 DevOps 文化的基石。

随着文化部分的解释，很容易回答这个问题。

是的，但是没有。

是的，因为公司会创建这样的职位。

不，因为在许多情况下，创建这样的职位名称的公司只会将 Ops 放在 DevOps 团队中。

这不是一个真正的工作角色。 它更多的是一种文化、方法和做事的心态。 组建团队并投入一些新工具不会给您带来 DevOps。

实现 DevOps 文化需要什么？

因为我们有共同的理解和共同的责任，传统的 Dev 需要向 Ops 学习，传统的 Ops 需要向 Dev 学习。

因为我们有新的实践和工具，所以每个人都需要不断地学习方法和新技术。

那么，如何实现 DevOps 文化呢？ 至此，这个问题可以自己回答了：

如何启用成长和学习心态

全球领先的研究和咨询公司 Gartner 在 2019 年预测，到 2022 年，75% 的 DevOps 计划将无法达到他们的预期； 到 2023 年，这一数字将增长到 90%。

原因：领导层使用的管理方法的局限性

领导支持是其成功的关键要素之一。

动机

让我告诉你另一个你可能会涉及到的故事：

我曾在许多初创公司工作过，而且这些初创公司中的许多似乎都共享一种“初创文化”：免费早餐和冰箱里的免费啤酒。

这背后的想法很简单：通过创造一个更受欢迎的环境，他们认为员工会更有动力。

我喜欢免费啤酒吗？ 当然。

这真的会激励我吗？ 不。

当我寻找下一个工作职位时，我不会以“冰箱里的免费啤酒”作为标准。 每月至少获得 10k 工资的人可能不会关心他们是否能获得价值 2 美元的啤酒。

冰箱里有免费啤酒不会激励你的团队不断学习和成长。

是什么激励团队成长

技术人员需要三件事来激励他们，它们是：

原因很容易理解：

当你对某一学科有全面的知识或技能，并有很大的成就时，你会更有动力。 就是这样，如果你在学校的工作得到更多的表扬，你下次就会更有决心把它做得更好。

团队学习速度很快，想要做更大的事情。 如果他们不断地被阻止和放慢速度，尤其是被领导层阻止，他们就会失去动力。 这就像以 50 公里/小时的速度驾驶保时捷跑车。 你有最好的工具，但你不能加快速度。 你不会喜欢那样开车，随着时间的推移你对开车失去兴趣。

团队需要知道他们为什么要做某事。 因为没有目的，一切都只是垃圾。

领导

一旦我们弄清楚了文化，以及激励团队实施文化的因素，就很容易弄清楚我们应该在领导力中寻求什么：我们需要必要的行为特征来授权和鼓励团队，而不是技术技能和专业知识，例如，如果领导者是云专业人士或 12 因素应用程序或你有什么。

领导力应该鼓励团队快速学习、快速尝试和快速失败，因为通过将失败视为学习机会，你可以以最快的方式学习。

领导力应该赋予团队权力，并赋予团队足够的自主权，让他们能够在没有事后怀疑的情况下做出决定。 我经常看到完全相反的情况，领导层甚至不知道他们在打击团队。

领导力应该提供明确的目标和关键指标，作为团队持续学习和成长心态的动力的一部分。

如果你回到我引用 AWS 对 DevOps 定义的开头，你会看到 AWS 提到了一些 Wikipedia 和 Atlassian 都没有提到的东西：

这种速度使组织能够更好地为客户服务并在市场上更有效地竞争。

是的，所有定义都提到了 DevOps 的好处是速度，但只有 AWS 解释了为什么需要这种速度：为您的客户服务。

我喜欢它，因为没有这个目的，DevOps 只是一个空洞的姿态。

在现代，越来越多的组织开始意识到 IT 架构不仅服务于业务，因为它是必须具备的，而且还为新业务和增长提供了更多可能性。 这就是 DevOps 的好处将带给您的地方。

概括

经过这么长时间的讨论，尝试定义 DevOps 是安全的：

责任共担模式

  • 不断成长和学习的心态
  • 来自 Dev 和 Ops 的技能组合
  • 最佳实践
  • 现代工具链

    • 这提高了交付速度，更快地获得客户反馈，并更快地调整您的产品和服务以更好地为您的客户服务。

    在下一篇或几篇文章中，我将讨论 DevOps 带来的好处。



    原文:

    Note: opinions are my own. They don't represent my current company or any previous companies I've worked for.

    Note 2: recently, my colleague published a blog post (in Chinese, here's the link if you are interested). I like this post, but I'd like to share more with you on the topic of DevOps. So I decided to publish a miniseries (7 episodes planned) on DevOps. Here's the first article.

    Note 3: this article is originally published on Jan 14, 2021, on Medium in my DevOps Chat publication named 4th Coffee. See it here if you are interested. This post is edited again on May 10th, 2022.

    I’ve been working as a DevOps engineer since 2016, and as of today in 2022, I'm still working on it (as the PMC of DevStream). In 2021, I was lucky enough to join AWS as a Senior (L6) DevOps (already left, though). But this article doesn’t represent AWS’s view; nor does it represent my current corporate view. It’s my personal opinion, which I learned and formulated over the years of hands-on experience in projects.

    In the beginning of 2021, I had the chance to talk on the DevOps topic — what it is, why we need it, and how to measure if we are doing it well or not. So I thought I should take that opportunity, form my thoughts into words, and share them with the community.

    In my professional career, I’ve worked in different businesses, including university research, search engine, government, internet, enterprise software, mobile, marketing business intelligence, logistics, automotive, pharmaceutical, insurance, consulting, etc.; and I’ve worked in different types of companies and projects: state-owned company, local internet giant, global IT enterprise, foreign start-ups, global traditional organizations, etc. And I have to say that I have learned and grown a lot during this great journey both as a person and as an engineer (full stack developer and DevOps engineer). I also attended a few conferences and meet-ups and even gave some talks in them, but I have never got the chance to summarize what I do and share the knowledge with a bigger audience, and that’s the reason I wanted to start this series.

    What is DevOps?

    Let Me Tell You a Story First.

    I have interviewed over 200 engineers in the past 3 years, and sometimes I’d like to ask this question. It’s probably meaningless; I only asked it because I want to see what it means to everybody.

    And in all the answers I got, it seems most people always start with this sentence: “that’s a good question.” Then the person starts to explain his thoughts.

    I’m not a native English speaker, but over the years, I’ve been gradually learning the fact that “that’s a good question” equals “I don’t know.”

    It’s a meaningless filler phrase to fill the awkward silence during conversations, and it buys you some time to organize your thought before you answer the question.

    Only on infrequent occasions would you hear this sentence because you really had asked an excellent question.

    I have also interviewed (as an interviewee) many companies over the last 5 years, both start-ups and large corporations. I also got asked about this quite often, at least more often than I had anticipated. Sometimes, during the conversation, you started to realize that the person or the company who asked this question didn’t really know the answer either; they asked this because they wanted to learn from you and see what new ideas you can bring to the team.

    Why Is It So Hard to Define DevOps?

    Why is this question hard to answer?

    Because, in my opinion, DevOps is a lot.

    Let’s Try to Figure It out

    Let me quote some definitions first before we discuss DevOps.

    Here I quote three definitions, and they are from Wikipedia (because it’s like an internet dictionary), AWS (because we all hear that AWS does good DevOps), and Atlassian (because as a DevOps, we probably use their tools a lot so they might know a thing or two):

    Wikipedia:

    DevOps is a set of practices that combines software development (Dev) and IT operations (Ops). It aims to shorten the systems development life cycle and provide continuous delivery with high software quality.[1][2] DevOps is complementary with agile software development; several DevOps aspects came from Agile methodology.

    (I know, there are a lot of links. You don't have to read all of them, but it's recommended.)

    AWS:

    DevOps is the combination of cultural philosophies, practices, and tools that increases an organization’s ability to deliver applications and services at high velocity: evolving and improving products at a faster pace than organizations using traditional software development and infrastructure management processes. This speed enables organizations to better serve their customers and compete more effectively in the market.

    Atlassian:

    DevOps is a set of practices that works to automate and integrate the processes between software development and IT teams, so they can build, test, and release software faster and more reliably.

    It seems there are some similarities, which are:

    And there are differences, of course, and let’s see them one by one:

    A Combination of Dev and Ops

    Wikipedia says it’s a combination of Dev and Ops.

    This basically says nothing because even if I knew nothing about this subject, I had already known from the word itself that it combines Dev and Ops.

    An excellent question here is: is DevOps really Dev + Ops?

    Yes, and No.

    Yes, because you would need the skillsets from both Dev and Ops.

    No, because simply putting two teams together wouldn’t get you there.

    Cultural Philosophies

    What AWS’s definition has and others don’t have, is the “cultural philosophies,” and I like it.

    Let’s say you have the tools and skillsets from both Dev and Ops. Would that be enough to enable you to achieve higher velocity?

    Probably not.

    The fact is, you already have Dev and Ops teams and the corresponding skillsets in your org. Then you pick up a new CI tool that is famous in the DevOps world. Would you be able to deliver faster already?

    I don’t think so.

    This is because how we do things is more important than what we have, and by definition, “how we do things” is the cultural part.

    Culture

    Culture is how human societies behave. What the customs are, what the norms are.

    For example:

    • What is right, what is wrong?
    • What is acceptable, and what isn’t?
    • How do you learn?
    • And more importantly, how do you encourage the team to learn?

    Culture can be highly technical. For example: how do you do code review? What types of code are acceptable, and what are not?

    And it can also be highly nontechnical. For example: what standards do we use for hiring and promoting?

    DevOps Culture

    Then, what exactly is the DevOps culture?

    You probably see this image (or something very similar to this) a lot. And to be honest, the DevOps culture is hidden in this picture:

    You don’t separate Dev and Ops. You have a shared understanding and shared responsibility. You take up the responsibility as a whole through collaboration and communication. And this is the cornerstone of the DevOps culture.

    Is DevOps a role?

    With the culture part explained, it’s easy to answer this question.

    Yes, but no.

    Yes, because companies create job titles like this.

    No, because companies that created the job titles like this only put Ops in the DevOps team in many cases.

    It’s not really a job role. It’s more of a culture, methodologies, and mindset to do things. Putting teams together and throwing in some new tools won’t give you DevOps.

    What is Required to Achieve the DevOps Culture?

    Because we have shared understanding and shared responsibilities, traditional Dev needs to learn from Ops, and traditional Ops need to learn from Dev.

    Because we have new practices and tools, everybody needs constant learning about methodologies and new technologies.

    So, how to achieve a DevOps culture? At this point, this question can answer itself:

    DevOps requires a “growth and continuous learning mindset” to succeed.

    How to Enable the Growth and Learning Mindset

    Gartner, the globally leading research and advisory company, predicted in 2019 that by 2022, 75% of DevOps initiatives would fail to meet their expectations; this number grows to 90% by 2023.

    Reason: limitations of management approaches used by leadership

    Leadership support is one of the critical elements of its success.

    Motivation

    Let me tell you another story that you probably will relate to:

    I’ve worked in many start-up companies, and it seems many of those start-ups share a “start-up culture”: free breakfast, and free beer in the fridge.

    The idea behind this is simple: by creating a more welcoming environment, they think employees will be more motivated.

    Do I love free beer? Of course.

    Will this really motivate me? No.

    When I search for my next job position, I will not use “free beer in the fridge” as a standard. People who get paid at least 10k per month probably wouldn’t care if they can get 2 dollars worth of beer.

    Having a free beer in the fridge won’t motivate your team to learn and grow continuously.

    What Motivates the Team to Grow

    Technical people need 3 things to be motivated, and they are:

    The reasons are easy to understand:

    When you have comprehensive knowledge or skill in a subject and have great accomplishments, you will be more motivated. It’s like that if you get more praise for your work in the school, you will be more determined to do it better next time.

    The team learns fast and wants to do greater things. If they are constantly blocked and slowed down, especially by leadership, they lose their motivation. It’s like driving a Porsche sportscar at 50km/h. You have the best tool, but you can’t speed up. You wouldn’t enjoy driving like that, and you lose interest in driving over time.

    The team needs to know why they are doing something. Because without a purpose, everything is just garbage.

    Leadership

    Once we figured out the culture, and what motivates the team to implement the culture, it’s easy to figure out what we should seek in leadership: we need necessary behavioral characteristics that empower and encourage the team, rather than technical skillsets and expertise, for example, if the leader is a professional in the cloud or 12-factor app or what have you.

    Leadership should encourage the team to learn fast, try fast, and fail fast because by embracing failure as a learning opportunity, you learn the fastest way.

    Leadership should empower the team, and give the team enough autonomy to make decisions free of second-guessing. I’ve seen exactly the opposite cases very often, and the leadership doesn't even know they are discouraging the team.

    Leadership should provide clear goals and key metrics as part of the motivation for the team’s continuous learning and growth mindset.

    IT Enables Business

    If you go back to the beginning where I quoted AWS’s definition of DevOps, you will see that AWS mentions something that neither Wikipedia nor Atlassian mentioned:

    This speed enables organizations to better serve their customers and compete more effectively in the market.

    Yes, all definitions mentioned DevOps' benefit being velocity, but only AWS explained why you need this velocity: to serve your customer.

    I like it because, without this purpose, DevOps is just an empty gesture.

    In modern days, more and more organizations start to realize that IT architecture not only serves the business because it’s a must-have but also enables more possibilities for new businesses and growth. And that’s where the DevOps’s benefit will bring you to.

    Summary

    After this long discussion, it’s safe to try to make a definition of DevOps:

    DevOps is a combination of:

    • a shared responsibility model
    • continuous growth and learning mindset
    • skillsets from both Dev and Ops
    • best practices
    • modern toolchains

    that increases velocity to deliver faster, get customer feedback faster, and adapt your product and service faster to serve your customers better.

    In the next article or a few articles, I will discuss the benefits that DevOps brings.

Items.Title: 你能写一本电子书吗？ | Can YOU Write an EBook?
Items.Description:
主要区别之一是自助出版在作家可以做的事情上提供了更多的控制和灵活性。 他们可以自由地与出版商合作，制定自己的营销策略，并建立自己的团队。 一方面，您不会处理印刷书籍。 有人自行出版吗？ 长期以来，印刷和分发实体副本的成本一直令人头疼。 但是电子书的出现在很大程度上消除了这个问题，并使每个人都可以访问数字出版。 这些是电子书，可以在亚马逊的 Kindle 等设备、其他平板电脑或智能手机，甚至电脑上阅读。

您不必依赖编辑或出版商来告诉您您的书是否会出版。 您不必依赖书店将您的书放在书架上。 您可以完全控制从封面设计到推广的整个过程。 你可以在出版之前尝试这本书。 你可以在亚马逊上预览你的书，看看人们是否已经在阅读它并购买副本。 您拥有自行出版的权力，这意味着您将能够控制有关您图书的所有内容。

以下是如何发布电子书的步骤：

  1. 写一本强大的书
  2. 创建 Amazon KDP 账户
  3. 格式化您的电子书以供发布
  4. 将您的电子书上传到 KDP
  5. 选择您的电子书发布日期
  6. 建立您的图书发布团队
  7. 为您的电子书大肆宣传
  8. 发布您的电子书！
  9. 在您的网站上强调它

返回目录

为什么以及如何写电子书？

以下是电子书的好处：

  • 它们的生产成本更低
  • 电子书更容易在线销售
  • 您可以增加您的博客及其收入
  • 被动收入
  • 电子书比实体书更能促进您的业务发展
  • 您可以将链接直接嵌入到您的网站和您销售的产品

以下是编写一本书并准备出版的总体步骤：

  1. 选择一个想法
  2. 想出一个好的书名和副标题
  3. 创建你的思维导图
  4. 从你的思维导图中写出一个完整的大纲
  5. 安排你的写作时间，完成你的书！
  6. 彻底的自我编辑
  7. 聘请编辑以确保完美
  8. 聘请书籍封面设计艺术家让您的书栩栩如生

返回目录

如何正确格式化电子书

常见错误

  • 避免硬缩进
  • 缩进与块段落
  • 避免句号后出现双空格
  • 谨慎使用连字符
  • 引号与撇号
  • 小心“回车”键
  • 使用样式功能

选择发布日期和团队

信不信由你，推出你的书实际上有好有坏。 一般来说，营销人员会说，寒假是你应该避免出版新书的时候，因为它会被其他品牌的广告过度饱和。 但是，您可以利用这段时间来发挥自己的优势。 你可以在假期开始推销你的书，然后当新的一年开始时，你真的能够获得你想要的牵引力。

那么，为什么作者真的需要一个优秀的发布团队呢？ 出版一本书对于作者和读者来说都是一个激动人心的时刻，但它也可能是一个压力大的事件。 事实是，在图书出版方面，有一点点很长的路要走，对于图书发行团队来说尤其如此。 你看，当你有很多人帮助你时，你会留下一个非常成功的图书发行印象。 就像那句老话，人多手轻。

发行团队对图书发行的成功起着不可或缺的作用。 作者需要一个坚实的狂热粉丝基础才能取得成功，而这样的团体不会神奇地出现。 建立忠实的粉丝群需要花费大量的时间和精力，所以如果您正在考虑出书，请不要忘记尽快建立您的发行团队！

您真正需要的是一群伟大的人，他们可以帮助您将书籍推向您无法达到的高度。

您的发布团队应由以下人员组成：

  • 喜欢你的书
  • 想帮助你
  • 对你的书很热心
  • 有某种追随者或在线存在
  • 是你和你作品的粉丝吗

返回目录

新书发行前要做什么

你不需要完美地执行它们，因为它们可以在以后进行调整。

但不要在没有完成以下关键任务的情况下推出你的书：

  • 图书说明 — 使用Kindlepreneur.com上的图书说明生成器创建您的图书说明。
  • 关键字 - 为您的书选择 7 个关键字。
  • 类别 — 在KDP 仪表板中为您的图书选择 2 个主要类别。 一旦您的书上线，您可以通过电子邮件向亚马逊发送电子邮件并请求将您的书放入另外八个类别。
  • 登陆页面 - 为您的图书创建登陆页面。 这可用于收集电子邮件地址并在图书发行前赠送一章（可选）。
  • 上传手稿 - 将您的手稿上传到 KDP。 使用 KDP 在线预览器校对您的图书。
  • 上传封面 — 将您的 Kindle 封面上传到 KDP。
  • 启动价格 - 将您的启动价格设置为 0.99。
  • 有声读物（可选）— 创建您的有声读物。 计划通过 Audible 或 ACX 发布您的图书。
  • 电子邮件 - 预先编写您将发送给您的发布团队的电子邮件。
  • 无论哪种方式，您的书都将在那里结束，因此值得设置一个帐户来与该书相关联。
  • 启动计划模型 - 列出您在启动的每一天所采取的具体行动步骤。 我在帖子的后面为此提供了一个模型。

返回目录

启动计划

您的启动计划将主要取决于：

  • 你的目标和目的。
  • 你的平台。 你的平台越大，影响力越大，你的发布就越大（也越多样化）。

在图书促销方面，您有很多选择。 您可以支付广告费用，在特定日期为您的图书安排促销活动，甚至可以撰写一篇有用的博客文章，介绍如何宣传您的图书。 但是，在 Facebook 等网站上存在很多竞争，要让您的书被人看到，最好的办法就是投资购买小说封面、引人入胜的书籍描述和朗朗上口的书名。 这三个基本要素是必须的，可以帮助您在竞争中脱颖而出。 很多时候，一个更好的封面、描述和标题会让你得到你无法达到的读者的注意。

以下是如何为发布电子书而大肆宣传：

  • 在博客文章中链接到您的书
  • 创建与您的图书主题相关的博客文章
  • 为您的书创建图形并放置在侧边栏和博客文章中
  • 创建要在网站首页上使用的图形
  • 创建电子邮件序列来销售您的图书
  • 不断寻找将您的书融入博客文章创意和社交媒体的方法

最佳图书发布推广网站：

注意：每个促销网站的价格因利基和类别而异。

请记住，每个网站的结果各不相同，您的表现在很大程度上取决于您图书的质量。 您仍然需要一些必需品：出色的书籍封面设计、引人入胜的书籍描述和引人注目的书名。

对于您的免费促销联系方式：

返回目录

快乐编码！



原文:

One of the key differences is that self-publishing offers more control and flexibility in what a writer can do. They’re free to collaborate with publishers, create their own marketing strategies, and build their own teams. You won’t be dealing with printed books, for one. Self-publishing anyone? The cost of printing and distributing physical copies has long been a headache. But the advent of eBooks has largely eliminated that problem and made digital publishing accessible to everyone. These are eBooks, which can be read on devices like Amazon’s Kindle, on another tablet or smartphone, or even on a computer.

You won't have to rely on an editor or publisher to tell you whether your book will be published. You won't have to rely on a bookstore to put your book on its shelves. You're in complete control of the entire process, from cover design to promotion. You can try the book before you publish it. You can preview your book on Amazon and see if people are already reading it and buying copies. You have the power of self-publishing, which means you will be able to control everything about your book.

Here are the steps for how to publish an ebook:

  1. Write a strong book
  2. Create an Amazon KDP account
  3. Format your ebook for publishing
  4. Upload your ebook to KDP
  5. Choose your ebook publish date
  6. Build your book launch team
  7. Create hype for your ebook
  8. Publish your ebook!
  9. Create emphasis for it on your site

Back to TOC

Why and How to Write an Ebook?

Here are the benefits of an ebook:

  • They’re cheaper to produce
  • Ebooks sell easier online
  • You can grow your blog and its income
  • Passive income
  • Ebooks can be used to grow your business more so than physical books
  • You can embed links directly to your site and products you sell

Here are the overall steps for writing a book and getting it ready for publication:

  1. Choose an idea
  2. Come up with a good book title and subtitle
  3. Create your mind map
  4. Write a thorough outline from your mind map
  5. Schedule your writing time and get your book done!
  6. Thoroughly self-edit
  7. Hire an editor to ensure perfection
  8. Hire a book cover design artist to bring your book to life

Back to TOC

How to Format the eBook Properly

Common Mistakes

  • Avoid hard indents
  • Indentations vs block paragraphs
  • Avoid double spaces after periods
  • Be cautious with hyphens
  • Quotes vs Apostrophes
  • Be careful with the “enter” key
  • Use the Style feature

Choosing the Launch Date and Team

Believe it or not, there are actually good and bad days to launch your book. Typically speaking, marketers say that the winter holiday season is when you should avoid publishing a new book because it will be super saturated with ads by other brands. You can, however, use this time to your advantage. You can start the process of marketing your book during the holidays, and then when the beginning of the new year rolls around, you'll really be able to gain the traction you want.

So, why is an author really going to need a good launch team? Launching a book can be an exciting time for both authors and readers alike, but it can also be a stressful event. The truth is that a little goes a long way in book publishing, and this is especially true when it comes to a book launch team. You see, when you have a lot of people helping you out, you’re left with the impression of a very successful book launch. It’s kind of like that old saying, many hands make light work.

Launch teams play such an integral role to the success of a book launch. Authors need a solid foundation of avid fans to succeed, and groups like that won't just magically appear. It takes a lot of time and effort to build a loyal fan-base, so if you're considering launching a book, don't forget to build your launch team as soon as possible!

What you really need is a great group of people who can help launch your book to heights you wouldn’t reach otherwise.

Your launch team should be composed of people who:

  • Love your book
  • Want to help you
  • Are very enthusiastic about your book
  • Have some sort of following or online presence
  • Are fans of you and your work

Back to TOC

What to do Before a Book Launch

You don’t need to carry them out with perfection since they can be tweaked later.

But don’t launch your book without doing these key tasks:

  • Book Description — Create your book description using the Book Description Generator at Kindlepreneur.com.
  • Keywords — Select 7 keywords for your book.
  • Categories — Choose 2 main categories for your book in the KDP dashboard. Once your book is live you can email Amazon and request to have your book put into eight additional categories.
  • Landing Page — Create a landing page for your book. This can be used to collect email addresses and give away a chapter of your book before its release (optional).
  • Upload Manuscript — Upload your manuscript to KDP. Proofread your book using the KDP online previewer.
  • Upload Cover — Upload your Kindle cover to KDP.
  • Launch Price — Set your launch price at 0.99.
  • Audiobook (Optional) — Get your audiobook created. Plan to release your book through Audible or ACX.
  • Emails — Pre-write emails that you’ll send to your launch team.
  • Goodreads — Goodreads account created and author profile setup. Your book will end up there either way, so it’s worth setting up an account to associate the book with.
  • Launch Plan Model — Map out the specific action steps you are taking for each day of your launch. I have provided a model for this further down the post.

Back to TOC

The Launch Plan

Your launch plan will depend largely on:

  • Your objectives and purpose.
  • Your platform. The bigger your platform and access to influences, the bigger (and more diverse) your launch.

When it comes to book promotion, you have a lot of options. You can pay for ads, schedule promotions for your book on specific dates, and even write a helpful blog post on how to promote your book. However, there is a lot of competition on sites like Facebook, and the best thing that you can do to get your books seen is to invest in a novel book cover, a compelling book description, and a catchy book title. These three essential elements are a must, and can help set you apart from the rest of your competition. Many times, a better cover, description, and title will allow you to get the attention of readers you wouldn’t have been able to reach otherwise.

Here’s how you can build hype for publishing your ebook:

  • Link to your book within blog posts
  • Create blog posts related to the topic of your book
  • Create graphics for your book and place in your sidebar and within blog posts
  • Create a graphic to use on the front page of your website
  • Create an email sequence to sell your book
  • Continuously look for ways to integrate your book into blog post ideas and on social media

The Best Book Launch Promotion Sites:

Note: The price for each promo site varies depending on the niche and category.

Keep in mind that results vary for each site and your performance will largely depend on your book’s quality. You still need the essentials: a great book cover design, a compelling book description, and an eye-catching book title.

For your free promo contact:

Back to TOC

Happy coding!

Items.Title: 如何使用 API Axios 请求更新 Google Cloud Run 实例 | How to Update Google Cloud Run Instance using an API Axios Request
Items.Description:
本教程介绍了如何使用 google cloud build docker 映像更新现有的 google cloud run 实例。

首先要将您的 docker 映像推送到 Google Cloud，您可以从本地代码库运行以下命令：
gcloud builds submit --timeout=2000s --machine-type=e2-highcpu-32 --tag gcr.io/PROJECT-NAME/IMAGE-NAME .

这将构建您的 docker 项目并将其作为映像存储在 GCP 容器注册表中。

然后要将映像部署为 Google Cloud 实例，您可以运行以下命令：
gcloud run deploy INSTANCE-NAME --image gcr.io/PROJECT-NAME/IMAGE-NAME --region us-east1 --platform managed --allow-unauthenticated --quiet

凉爽的！ 现在我们的 docker 项目正在 Cloud Run 中运行。

就我而言，我为不同的网站主题使用不同的代码库，并使用 docker 构建它们。 因此，我使用第一个命令将所有主题提交到 Google Cloud，但在从前端应用程序切换主题时，我需要一种编程方式用新的 docker 映像覆盖我的云运行实例。

通常您可以运行gcloud run deploy CLI 命令，但我需要将 CLI 命令转换为我添加到后端云功能的 axios API 请求。

这是我做到的：

 try { async function updateTheme() { let token: any const jwtClient = new google.auth.JWT(firebaseClientEmail, "", firebaseKey, [ 'https://www.googleapis.com/auth/cloud-platform', ]) await jwtClient.authorize(async function (err: any, _token: any) { if (err) { console.log("JWT ERROR: ", err) return err } else { token = _token.access_token.split('.') token = token[0] + '.' + token[1] + '.' + token[2] const deploySteps = [ { name: 'gcr.io/cloud-builders/gcloud', args: [ 'run', 'deploy', `${name}`, '--image', `gcr.io/${googleCloudProject}/theme-${theme}`, '--region', 'us-east1', '--allow-unauthenticated', '--platform', 'managed', '--quiet', ], }, ] const deployRevisions = async () => { await axios({ method: "post", url: `https://cloudbuild.googleapis.com/v1/projects/${googleCloudProject}/builds`, headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}`, }, data: { steps: deploySteps, timeout: "1200s", }, }) .catch(function (error: any) { console.log("ERROR UPDATING THEME: ", error) return }) .then(function (response: any) { console.log("SUCCESSFULLY DEPLOYED THEME UPDATE") }) } if (token) { deployRevisions() } else { console.log("MISSING TOKEN") } } }) } await updateTheme() } catch (e) { console.log("tried updating theme but something went wrong") return }

上述代码块利用 Cloud Build API 在 Google Cloud 中使用 docker 映像覆盖云运行实例。 它还会获取要在请求中使用的 Google Cloud 身份验证代码。

当您希望更新云运行实例时，将其放在一个函数中并简单地传入图像名称



原文:

This tutorial is explains how to update an existing google cloud run instance using a google cloud build docker image.

First to push your docker image to Google Cloud you can run the following from your local codebase:
gcloud builds submit --timeout=2000s --machine-type=e2-highcpu-32 --tag gcr.io/PROJECT-NAME/IMAGE-NAME .

This will build your docker project and store it as an image in GCP container registry.

Then to deploy the image as a Google Cloud instance you can run this command:
gcloud run deploy INSTANCE-NAME --image gcr.io/PROJECT-NAME/IMAGE-NAME --region us-east1 --platform managed --allow-unauthenticated --quiet

Cool! Now our docker project is running in Cloud Run.

In my case I use have different code bases for different website themes and build them using docker. So I used the first command to submit all my themes to Google Cloud, but I needed a programmatic way to overwrite my cloud run instance with a new docker image when switching between themes from my frontend app.

Usually you could run the gcloud run deploy CLI command but I needed to convert the CLI command to an axios API request I add to a cloud function in my backend.

Here's I did it:

try {
async function updateTheme() {
  let token: any
  const jwtClient = new google.auth.JWT(firebaseClientEmail, "", firebaseKey, [
    'https://www.googleapis.com/auth/cloud-platform',
  ])

  await jwtClient.authorize(async function (err: any, _token: any) {
    if (err) {
      console.log("JWT ERROR: ", err)
      return err
    } else {
      token = _token.access_token.split('.')
      token = token[0] + '.' + token[1] + '.' + token[2]

      const deploySteps = [
        {
          name: 'gcr.io/cloud-builders/gcloud',
          args: [
            'run',
            'deploy',
            `${name}`,
            '--image',
            `gcr.io/${googleCloudProject}/theme-${theme}`,
            '--region',
            'us-east1',
            '--allow-unauthenticated',
            '--platform',
            'managed',
            '--quiet',
          ],
        },
      ]

      const deployRevisions = async () => {
        await axios({
          method: "post",
          url: `https://cloudbuild.googleapis.com/v1/projects/${googleCloudProject}/builds`,
          headers: {
            Authorization: `Bearer ${token}`,
          },
          data: {
            steps: deploySteps,
            timeout: "1200s",
          },
        })
          .catch(function (error: any) {
            console.log("ERROR UPDATING THEME: ", error)
            return
          })
          .then(function (response: any) {
            console.log("SUCCESSFULLY DEPLOYED THEME UPDATE")
          })
      }

      if (token) {
        deployRevisions()
      } else {
        console.log("MISSING TOKEN")
      }
    }
  })
}

await updateTheme()

} catch (e) {
    console.log("tried updating theme but something went wrong")
    return
}

The above chunk of code utilizes the Cloud Build API to overwrite a cloud run instance with a docker image in Google Cloud. It fetches the Google Cloud authentication code to use in the request as well.

Place it in a function and simple pass in the image name when you wish to update your cloud run instance

Items.Author: ben

Items.Title: 初学者的抽象介绍 | Introduction to abstraction for beginners
Items.Description:
在面向对象编程中，抽象是一个关键概念，它允许您创建表示对象的一般概念而不是该对象的特定实例的类和方法。 这样做的目的是向用户隐藏实现细节。 这篇博客将解释什么是抽象，它是如何在 C# 中实现的，以及抽象的优势。

面向对象编程中的抽象对用户隐藏了不重要和复杂的细节，只显示了重要信息。 这意味着用户无需了解程序的复杂性即可使用它； 抽象使得在不了解底层实现细节的情况下使用复杂对象变得容易。 当你开车时，你不需要知道发动机是如何从 A 点到达 B 点的。你可以上车转动钥匙，剩下的事情由发动机来处理。 这是现实世界中抽象的一个例子。 抽象常常与封装相混淆。 但是，封装和抽象是两个不同的概念。 封装是面向对象编程中的另一个关键概念，它指的是将数据与操作该数据的方法捆绑在一起，并涉及限制对数据和方法的访问，这有时被称为数据隐藏。 抽象是一种向用户隐藏对象的实现细节的方法，而封装是一种向用户隐藏数据本身的方法。

抽象类如何提供抽象？


抽象类是不能被实例化的类，这意味着你不能创建抽象类类型的对象。 但是，抽象类可以被另一个类继承。 继承抽象类时，继承类必须为基类的所有抽象成员提供实现。 结果是基类通过有效地添加抽象层（抽象类）来向继承类隐藏这些成员的实现细节。

如何在 C# 中实现抽象

如前所述，抽象是在类和方法中实现的。 在 C# 中，您可以使用关键字“abstract”创建一个抽象类，它可以包含抽象和非抽象成员。 抽象类是无法实例化的类，这意味着您无法创建该类型的对象。 抽象方法没有主体，它们必须在派生类中实现。 非抽象方法是具有主体的方法，无需在派生类中实现即可调用它们。 在下面的示例中，我们创建了一个名为“Animal”的抽象类，其中包含一个抽象方法和一个非抽象方法：

如您所见，“Animal”类有一个名为“MakeNoise”的抽象方法和一个名为“Sleep”的非抽象方法。 您还可以使用关键字“abstract”在非抽象类中创建抽象方法。 在以下示例中，我们在“Animal”类中创建了一个名为“MakeNoise”的抽象方法：

如您所见，“Animal”类有一个名为“MakeNoise”的抽象方法和一个名为“Sleep”的非抽象方法。 创建抽象方法时，必须为派生类中的方法提供主体。 您可以使用关键字“override”来做到这一点。 在下面的示例中，我们创建了一个名为“Dog”的派生类，它覆盖了“MakeNoise”方法：

如您所见，“Dog”类通过为该方法提供一个主体来覆盖“MakeNoise”方法。 当您在“Dog”类型的对象上调用“MakeNoise”方法时，“Woof！” 将显示消息。

抽象的优点是什么？

抽象在软件开发中有几个好处：

  • 抽象允许您创建可重用的代码。
  • 抽象使您的代码更高效且更易于维护。
  • 抽象可以使您的代码更加灵活和可扩展。
  • 抽象可以帮助您防止代码重复。
  • 抽象可以使您的代码更易于理解和使用。


原文:

In object-oriented programming, abstraction is a critical concept that allows you to create classes and methods that represent the general concept of an object rather than specific instances of that object. The purpose of this is to hide implementation details from users. This blog will explain what abstraction is, how it is implemented in C# and the advantages of abstraction.

*What is abstraction in object-orientated programming? *

Abstraction in object-orientated programming hides unimportant and complex details from the user and only displays the vital information. This means a user doesn't need to understand the complexities of a program in order to use it; abstraction makes it easy to use complex objects without understanding the underlying implementation details. When you drive a car, you don't need to know how the engine works to get from point A to point B. You can get in the car and turn the key, and the engine will take care of the rest. This is an example of abstraction in the real world. Abstraction is often confused with encapsulation. However, encapsulation and abstraction are two different concepts. Encapsulation is another key concept in object-oriented programming that refers to bundling data with the methods that operate on that data and involves restricting access to the data and methods, which is sometimes referred to as data hiding. Abstraction is a way of hiding the implementation details of an object from the user, whereas encapsulation is a way of hiding the data itself from the user.

How do abstract classes provide abstraction?


An abstract class is a class that cannot be instantiated, which means that you cannot create an object of the abstract class type. An abstract class can, however, be inherited by another class. When an abstract class is inherited, the inheriting class must provide implementations for all of the abstract members of the base class. The result is that the base class hides the implementation details of those members from the inheriting class by effectively adding a layer ( the abstract class) of abstraction.

How to implement abstraction in C#

As mentioned previously, abstraction is implemented in classes and methods. In C#, you can create an abstract class using the keyword "abstract" and it can contain abstract and non-abstract members. An abstract class is a class that cannot be instantiated, meaning you cannot create an object of that type. Abstract methods do not have a body, and they must be implemented in a derived class. The non-abstract methods are methods that have a body, and they can be called without having to implement them in a derived class. In the following example, we have created an abstract class called "Animal" with one abstract method and one non-abstract method:

As you can see, the "Animal" class has an abstract method called "MakeNoise" and a non-abstract method called "Sleep". The "MakeNoise" method does not have a body, so it must be implemented in a derived class. The "Sleep" method has a body, so it can be called without having to implement it in a derived class. You can also create an abstract method in a non-abstract class by using the keyword "abstract ". In the following example, we have created an abstract method called "MakeNoise" in the "Animal" class:

As you can see, the "Animal" class has an abstract method called "MakeNoise" and a non-abstract method called "Sleep". The "MakeNoise" method does not have a body, so it must be implemented in a derived class. The "Sleep" method has a body, so it can be called without having to implement it in a derived class.
When you create an abstract method, you must provide a body for the method in a derived class. You can do this by using the keyword "override ". In the following example, we have created a derived class called "Dog" that overrides the "MakeNoise" method:

As you can see, the "Dog" class overrides the "MakeNoise" method by providing a body for the method. When you call the "MakeNoise" method on an object of type "Dog", the "Woof!" message will be displayed.

What are the advantages of abstraction?

There are several benefits of abstraction in software development:

  • Abstraction allows you to create reusable code.
  • Abstraction makes your code more efficient and easier to maintain.
  • Abstraction can make your code more flexible and extensible.
  • Abstraction can help you prevent code duplication.
  • Abstraction can make your code more understandable and easier to use.

Items.Title: Jest + Typescript 减去 TS-Jest | Jest + Typescript minus TS-Jest
Items.Description:
酸性编码器

发表于当您尝试将typescript与jest一起使用时， ts-jest是一个流行的包。

但是最近困扰我的一个问题是，未覆盖的行统计不正确，我的测试覆盖率下降了 10+%。

谷歌搜索后，没有任何有用的东西出现，经过一些故障排除后，我发现是ts-jest导致了问题

所以我决定改用 babel 进行编译

安装依赖项

npm i -D @babel/preset-env @babel/preset-typescript typescript jest

设置 npm 脚本

"scripts" : { "test" : "jest" ,
}

创建一个jest.config.js

 /** @type {import('ts-jest/dist/types').InitialOptionsTsJest} */ module . exports = { testEnvironment : ' node ' , roots : [ ' <rootDir>/src ' ], testMatch : [ ' **/__tests__/**/*.+(ts|js) ' , ' **/?(*.)+(spec|test).+(ts|js) ' ], transform : { ' ^.+ \\ .(js|ts)$ ' : ' babel-jest ' , }, moduleDirectories : [ ' node_modules ' , ' src ' ], collectCoverage : true , collectCoverageFrom : [ ' **/*.{js,ts} ' , ' !**/*.d.ts ' ], }

创建一个babel.config.js

 module . exports = { presets : [ ' @babel/preset-typescript ' , [ ' @babel/preset-env ' , { targets : { node : ' current ' , }, }, ], ], }

你不需要安装babel-jest ，它自带jest

最后npm test和繁荣，你的 100% 测试覆盖率回来了。

Jan Schenk (he/him) - Jan Schenk (他/他) - May 9 5月9日



原文:

Acid Coder

Posted on May 12

ts-jest is a popular package when you try to use typescript with jest.

But one issue that is bugging me recently is, the uncovered lines stat is not correct, my test coverage dropped by 10+%.

After googling, nothing useful come up, and after some troubleshooting, I found out that it was ts-jest that causing the issue

so I decided to use babel for compilation instead

install dependencies

npm i -D @babel/preset-env @babel/preset-typescript typescript jest

setup npm script

"scripts": { "test": "jest",
}

create a jest.config.js

/** @type {import('ts-jest/dist/types').InitialOptionsTsJest} */ module.exports = { testEnvironment: 'node', roots: ['<rootDir>/src'], testMatch: ['**/__tests__/**/*.+(ts|js)', '**/?(*.)+(spec|test).+(ts|js)'], transform: { '^.+\\.(js|ts)$': 'babel-jest', }, moduleDirectories: ['node_modules', 'src'], collectCoverage: true, collectCoverageFrom: ['**/*.{js,ts}', '!**/*.d.ts'],
}

create a babel.config.js

module.exports = { presets: [ '@babel/preset-typescript', [ '@babel/preset-env', { targets: { node: 'current', }, }, ], ],
}

you don't need to install babel-jest, it comes with jest

finally npm test and boom, your 100% test coverage is back.

Jan Schenk (he/him) - May 9

Items.Description:
工厂
命题
问题
解决方案
埃斯特鲁图拉
实施
参考文献

命题

问题

解决方案

埃斯特鲁图拉

  1. 实施

    abstract class Creator { public abstract factoryMethod (): Product ; public someOperation (): string { const product = this . factoryMethod (); console . log ( product . opertation ()); } }
     class ConcreteCreator1 extends Creator { public factoryMethod (): Product { return new ConcreteProduct1 (); } } class ConcreteCreator2 extends Creator { public factoryMethod (): Product { return new ConcreteProduct2 (); } }

    参考文献

    • https://refactoring.guru/pt-br/design-patterns/factory-method https://refactoring.guru/pt-br/design-patterns/factory-method


原文:

Factory
Propósito
Problema
Solução
Estrutura
Implementação
Referencias

Propósito

Fornecer uma interface para criar objetos em uma superclasse, mas permitir que as subclasses alterem o tipo de objetos que serão criados

Problema

Se você tem um código muito acoplado a uma á uma classe em especifico, caso um dia precise adicionar uma outra classe vai ter problemas, por exemplo, uma empresa de logistica que usa somente caminhões, e o sistema está fortemente acoplado a classe de caminhão, caso algum dia queira ser implementada a classe navio isso vai gerar uma refatoração gigantesca no código

Solução

O padrão factory sugere substituir as chamadas diretas de construção de objetos, nesse caso caminhão para um método factory e ai esse factory vai ficar responsavel por criar e retornar os objetos que nesse caso chamamos de produtos

Nesse exemplo do site refactoring guru, podemos ver que temos uma classe Logistics que contem um método createTransport que é uma factory, as classes RoadLogistics e SeaLogistics implementam essa classe Logistics e modificam o createTransport para retornar um caminhão e um návio respectivamente

Porem existe uma limitação, as subclasses só podem retornar tipos diferentes de produtos caso eles implemente uma mesma interface em comum. Isso ajuda para que quando o cliente faça uma chamada ele apenas precisa saber qual a interface desses produtos e pode tratar todos da mesma forma pois ele sabe que todos vão ter os mesmos métodos

Estrutura

  1. O Produto declara a interface que que os objetos vão implementar

  2. O produto concreto A e B são diferentes implementações da interface Produto

  3. A classe Creator tem um método factory que cria um produto do tipo Produto
    (Apesar do nome a principal responsabilidade da classe creator não é criar produtos)

  4. Criadores concretos sobescrevem a classe Creator para retornar diferente produtos no seus metodos factory

Implementação 

abstract class Creator { public abstract factoryMethod(): Product; public someOperation(): string { const product = this.factoryMethod(); console.log(product.opertation()); }
}

class ConcreteCreator1 extends Creator { public factoryMethod(): Product { return new ConcreteProduct1(); }
} class ConcreteCreator2 extends Creator { public factoryMethod(): Product { return new ConcreteProduct2(); }
}

os concrete creators vão extender o Creator e mudar apenas o método factory

interface Product { operation(): string;
} class ConcreteProduct1 implements Product { public operation(): string { return '{Result of the ConcreteProduct1}'; }
} class ConcreteProduct2 implements Product { public operation(): string { return '{Result of the ConcreteProduct2}'; }
}

function clientCode(creator: Creator) { console.log(creator.someOperation());
}

a partir dai o clientCode precisa apenas receber qualquer classe que extende o creator e ele tem certeza que essa classe vai ter o método someOperation que pode fazer coisas diferentes como por exemplo o no exemplo do barco e caminhão ambos podem ter o método delivery que funcionam de formas diferentes mas isso não import para o clientCode

Referencias

  • https://refactoring.guru/pt-br/design-patterns/factory-method

发表于可以将任意数量的事件处理程序添加到单个元素，而不会覆盖现有的事件处理程序。

以下是一些最常见的事件类型和事件名称：

  • 鼠标事件：click、dblclick、mousedown、mouseup、contextmenu、mouseout、mousewheel、mouseover。

  • 触摸事件：touchstart、touchend、touchmove、touchcancel

  • 键盘事件：keydown、keyup、keypress。

  • 表单事件：焦点、模糊、更改、提交

  • 窗口事件：调整大小、滚动、加载、卸载、hashchange。

触摸事件在智能手机、平板电脑和触摸屏笔记本电脑等支持触摸的设备上触发。 大多数浏览器和设备都会触发鼠标事件。 键盘事件（keydown，keyup）：

  • 表单事件（更改，提交）：

    • 不可用mako - 迈克尔·安德鲁扎—— May 11 5月11日



原文:

Aftab Sidhu

Posted on May 12

The addEventListener() is an inbuilt function in JavaScript which takes the event to listen for, and a second argument to be called whenever the described event gets fired. Any number of event handlers can be added to a single element without overwriting existing event handlers.

Here are some of the most common event types and event names:

  • Mouse Events: click, dblclick, mousedown, mouseup, contextmenu, mouseout, mousewheel, mouseover.

  • Touch Events: touchstart, touchend, touchmove, touchcancel

  • Keyboard Events: keydown, keyup, keypress.

  • Form Events: focus, blur, change, submit

  • Window Events: resize, scroll, load, unload, hashchange.

Touch events are triggered on touch-enabled devices such as smartphones, tablets, and touch-screen laptops. Mouse events are triggered on the majority of all browsers and devices. The MouseEvent interface represents events that occur due to the user interacting with a pointing device.

The click event: The onclick event occurs when the user clicks on an element.

The dblclick event: ondblclick event occurs when the user double-clicks on an element.

Mousedown & Mouseup events:A pointing device button is pressed/released on an element.

Mouseout event: A pointing device is moved off the element that has the listener attached.

Keyboard events(keydown, keyup):

  • Keydown: Any key is pressed.

  • keyup: Any key is released.

Form Events(change, submit):

  • change: Event that is fired for input, select, and textarea elements when an alteration to the element’s value is done by the user.

  • submit: The submit button is pressed.

notsoavailablemako - May 11

Michael Andreuzza - May 11

Items.Title: 在开发中使用 Foreman 启动服务 | Using Foreman to start services in development
Items.Description:
在任何开发环境中，通常都需要启动许多后台服务才能正确编码。

我不喜欢让所有必要的服务一直在我的机器上运行以节省资源，所以我只在需要时才启动它们； 并一个接一个地启动它们，这是太多的工作。

工头来救援！ 例如，每次我开始在项目中编码时，我都需要运行所有这些服务：

  • 雷迪斯
  • 打包程序
  • 导轨服务器
  • 铁路工人

这个过程的自动化很简单。 让我们首先创建一个Procfile并将其保存在项目根目录中。

./Procfile

 postgres: postgres -D /usr/local/var/postgres redis: redis-server /usr/local/etc/redis.conf webpacker: bundle exec bin/webpack-dev-server rails: bundle exec rails server worker: bundle exec rails jobs:work

如您所见，这很容易。 只需遵循这个简单的规则：

 service_name: command to run it

然后输入：

 $ foreman start 20:30:42 postgres.1 | started with pid 42458 20:30:42 rails.1 | started with pid 42459 20:30:42 worker.1 | started with pid 42460 20:30:42 redis.1 | started with pid 42461 20:30:42 webpack.1 | started with pid 42462

你准备好了！

感谢David Dollar提供的这个很棒的宝石和



原文:

In any development environment it's usually needed to start many background services to be able to code properly.

I don't like to keep all necessary services running in my machine all the time to save resources, so I start them only when needed; and starting them one-by-one it's too much work.

Comes Foreman to the rescue! Foreman is a gem (for Ruby, but it already ported to many others languages) that will load a Procfile and start/stop the services configured by demand.

For example, every time I start coding in a project, I need to run all those services:

  • PostgreSQL
  • Redis
  • Webpacker
  • Rails server
  • Rails worker

Automation of this procedure is simple. Lets first create a Procfile and save it on the project root directory.

./Procfile

postgres: postgres -D /usr/local/var/postgres
redis: redis-server /usr/local/etc/redis.conf
webpacker: bundle exec bin/webpack-dev-server
rails: bundle exec rails server
worker: bundle exec rails jobs:work

As you can see, it's easy. Just follow this simple rule:

service_name: command to run it

Then type:

$ foreman start

20:30:42 postgres.1 | started with pid 42458
20:30:42 rails.1    | started with pid 42459
20:30:42 worker.1   | started with pid 42460
20:30:42 redis.1    | started with pid 42461
20:30:42 webpack.1  | started with pid 42462

and you are ready to go!

Thanks David Dollar for this awesome gem and

Items.Title: 如何在 JavaScript 中给句子命名 | How to Titleize a Sentence in JavaScript
Items.Description:
我们最近在工作中遇到了一个场景，我们经历了重做应用程序中使用的所有电子邮件模板的过程。 我们重新设计了 300 多个模板。 在我们完成后，有人要求我们将主题行更改为标题大小写，就像您在打印标题中所做的那样。 不想手动更改所有 300 多个模板，我编写了这个自动处理该过程的快速函数。

什么是标题案例

乍一看，我们可能希望主题行中每个单词的首字母大写，而单词的其余部分小写。

嗯，这几乎是正确的。 我们不希望某些单词被大写。 像a 、 an or 这样的词and我们希望保持小写。

这是我创建的为我们处理此转换的快速函数。

首先，我创建了一组我们希望在电子邮件主题中保持小写的单词。

 const small_words = ['a', 'an', 'and', 'as', 'at', 'but', 'by', 'for', 'if', 'in', 'into', 'nor', 'of', 'on', 'or', 'out', 'so', 'the', 'to', 'up', 'yet', 'and', 'as', 'at', 'but', 'by', 'for', 'if', 'in', 'into', 'nor', 'of', 'on', 'or', 'out', 'so', 'the', 'to', 'up', 'yet'];

接下来我创建了一个函数，它将主题行的文本传递给它。 我会将这个字符串分解成一个数组，主题中的每个单词都是数组中的一个条目。 现在我有了一个数组，我可以遍历每个单词。 如果单词与small_words数组中的条目匹配，那么我将其转换为小写。 否则，我将单词的第一个字母大写，并将单词的其余部分小写。

我必须考虑一个例外，那就是主题文本中的第一个字母。 不管第一个单词是什么，我都希望它的第一个字母大写。

有了这些要求，这是我的功能：

 const titleize = (title) => { return title.split(' ').map((word, index) => { if (index !== 0 && small_words.includes(word)) { return word.toLowerCase(); } else { return word[0].toUpperCase() + word.slice(1).toLowerCase(); } }).join(' '); }

为了测试这个功能，我添加了 3 个标题和 console.log 新标题。

 console.log(titleize('notes on a scandal')); // Notes on a Scandal console.log(titleize('a good man is hard to find')); // A Good Man Is Hard to Find console.log(titleize('midnight in the garden of good and evil')); // Midnight in the Garden of Good and Evil

您将看到主题行的每个测试的输出。

如果您需要根据打印标题格式对字符串进行标题化，则可以使用此功能。 您需要做的就是编辑small_words数组以包含您的单词列表以保持小写。

马克·加西亚激流 - May 11 5月11日



原文:

We recently ran into a scenario at work where we went through the process to redo all the email templates used in our application. There were over 300+ templates that we redesigned. After we finished somebody requested that we change the subject line to be title cased like you would in a print headline. Not wanting to manually change all 300+ templates, I wrote this quick function that handled the process automatically.

What is Title Case

At first glance it might sound like we wanted the first letter of every word in a the subject line to be uppercase and the remainder of the word to be lowercase.

Well that is almost correct. We don't want certain words to be capitalized. Words like a, an or and we wanted to remain lowercase.

Here is the quick function I created that handled this transition for us.

First I created an array of words that we wanted to remain lowercase in the subject of the email.

const small_words = ['a', 'an', 'and', 'as', 'at', 'but', 'by', 'for', 'if', 'in', 'into', 'nor', 'of', 'on', 'or', 'out', 'so', 'the', 'to', 'up', 'yet', 'and', 'as', 'at', 'but', 'by', 'for', 'if', 'in', 'into', 'nor', 'of', 'on', 'or', 'out', 'so', 'the', 'to', 'up', 'yet'];

Next I created a function that would have the text of the subject line passed into it. I would break this string into an array with each word in the subject an entry in the array. Now that I have an array I can loop over every word. If the word matches an entry in the small_words array then I convert it to lowercase. Otherwise I capitalize the first letter of the word and lowercase the remainder of the word.

There is one exception that I have to account for and that is the first letter in the subject text. Regardless of what is the first word I want it to have the first letter capitalized.

With those requirements, here is my function:

const titleize = (title) => {
  return title.split(' ').map((word, index) => {
    if (index !== 0 && small_words.includes(word)) {
      return word.toLowerCase();
    } else {
      return word[0].toUpperCase() + word.slice(1).toLowerCase();
    }
  }).join(' ');
}

To test out the function I added 3 titles and console.log the new title.

console.log(titleize('notes on a scandal'));  // Notes on a Scandal
console.log(titleize('a good man is hard to find')); // A Good Man Is Hard to Find
console.log(titleize('midnight in the garden of good and evil')); // Midnight in the Garden of Good and Evil

You will see the output for each test of a subject line.

If you ever need to titleize a string based on print title formatting, you can use this function. All you would need to do is edit the small_words array to include your list of words to remain lowercase.

Dumb Down Demistifying Dev - May 11

Marc Garcia Torrent - May 11
Items.Author: ratracegrad

Items.Title: Elixir: Qual a diferençade usar so preload e preload com join? | Elixir: Qual a diferença de usar só preload e preload com join?
Items.Description:
示例 só com 预加载

from ( p in Post , preload: [ :comments ]) |> Repo . all ()

瓯

Post |> preload ([ :comments ]) |> Repo . all

结果：

蓬托斯观察者：

  1. 瓯

    Post |> join ( :inner , [ p ], c in assoc ( p , :comments )) |> preload ([ :comments ]) |> Repo . all () # ou Post |> join ( :inner , [ p ], c in Comment , on: p . id == c . post_id ) |> preload ([ :comments ]) |> Repo . all ()

    结果：

    蓬托斯观察者：

    1. 结果：

      结论

      Apenas o preload buscará todas as postagens do banco de dados e, em seguida, fará uma consulta separada retornando todos os comentários associados às postagens fornecidas. Apenas o preload buscará todas as postagens do banco de dados e, em seguida, fará uma Consulta separada retornando todos os comentários associados às postagens fornecidas。 Já usando preload com join em vez de emitir uma consulta separada para buscar comentários, o Ecto buscará postagens e comentários em uma única consulta e, em seguida, fará uma passagem separada associando cada comentário à postagem pai. Já usando preload com join em vez de emitir uma Consulta separada para buscar comentários, o Ecto buscará postagens e comentários em uma única Consulta e, em seguida, fará uma Passagem separada associando cada comentário à postagem pai。



原文:

Vamos usar o projeto do post Sintaxes de consulta em Elixir: pipe X palavras-chave para fazermos uns testes com os comentários e observar o comportamento de usar só preload e preload com join.

Vamos buscar os posts com seus comentários:

Exemplo só com preload 

from(p in Post, preload: [:comments]) |> Repo.all()

ou

Post
|> preload([:comments])
|> Repo.all

resultado:

O exemplo acima buscou todas as postagens do banco de dados e, em seguida, fez uma consulta separada retornando todos os comentários associados às postagens fornecidas. Os comentários foram processados ​​e associados a cada post sendo retornados no campo comments.

Pontos a observar:

  1. Nenhum registro apareceu duplicado.
  2. Todos os posts apareceram, mesmo os posts sem comentários.

Exemplo com preload e join

Muitas vezes, você pode querer que postagens e comentários sejam selecionados e filtrados na mesma consulta. Para esses casos, você pode dizer explicitamente que uma junção existente seja pré-carregada no conjunto de resultados:

Repo.all( from p in Post, join: c in assoc(p, :comments), preload: [comments: c] )

ou

Post
|> join(:inner, [p], c in assoc(p, :comments))
|> preload([:comments])
|> Repo.all() # ou Post
|> join(:inner, [p], c in Comment, on: p.id == c.post_id)
|> preload([:comments])
|> Repo.all()

resultado:

resultado similar a uma query usando SQL:

Pontos a observar:

  1. Como o post de id 1 tinha dois comentários, ele apareceu duplicado, similar a consulta com SQL.
  2. Apareceram apenas os posts que possuem comentários.

Podemos corrigir a duplicação usando o distinct:

Repo.all( from p in Post, distinct: true, join: c in assoc(p, :comments), preload: [comments: c] )

Post
|> distinct(true)
|> join(:inner, [p], c in assoc(p, :comments))
|> preload([:comments])
|> Repo.all()

resultado:

Agora temos apenas os posts que possuem comentários.

Conclusão

Apenas o preload buscará todas as postagens do banco de dados e, em seguida, fará uma consulta separada retornando todos os comentários associados às postagens fornecidas. Já usando preload com join em vez de emitir uma consulta separada para buscar comentários, o Ecto buscará postagens e comentários em uma única consulta e, em seguida, fará uma passagem separada associando cada comentário à postagem pai.

Items.Title: 我们的开源项目获得了资助！ | Our open-source project got funded!
Items.Description:
迪玛格罗斯曼

发表于嗨社区，我们有令人兴奋的消息要分享！

我已经写过这个想法的起源，你可以在这里阅读：构建第一个开源通知基础设施。

多亏了这个梦幻般的社区令人难以置信的兴趣，我们的 Github 项目变得流行起来，从 200 颗星增长到 2.2K 星，今天已经超过 3,500 颗星。
我们不能对社区表示足够的感谢。

什么是诺武？

它解决了开发人员从头开始构建和维护通知系统的痛苦。 这可以是一个应用内通知中心（就像你可能从 Facebook 等平台知道的那样），用于通过多个交付渠道发送的交易消息。

我们的目标是解决从摘要引擎到高级工作流管理（包括用户偏好、时区感知等）的大量用例。

最好的部分是——我们在开源社区的难以置信的帮助和参与下构建它。 一起。

最近，我们筹集了 660 万美元的资金。 这意味着我们可以全职构建这个项目。 梦想成真。 它还将使我们能够发展它，并在考虑通知和基于产品的通信时成为开发人员事实上的解决方案。

凭借我们的新资金，Novu 招募了我们的许多贡献者在 Novu 上全职工作，现在正致力于创建最好的 OSS 通知基础设施。

那么自资助以来发生了什么？

  • 我们发布了用于管理通知的管理控制面板 (npx novu init) 耶！。
  • 我们创建了一个新网站，更新了所有新功能。
  • 我们开始与贡献者和资助项目密切合作。
  • 我们赞助了活动。
  • 发布了我们的多环境支持
  • 以 Iframe 或 React 组件的形式发布了我们的应用内通知中心
  • 发布更多供应商和供应商仪表板
  • 添加了新的团队协作和邀请
  • 发布了新的监控和可观察性页面
  • 使用 CLI 创建了一个很棒的注册流程 :)
  • 全部开源！！！

未来？

  • 支持所有可能的通知渠道：Direct、Push 等。
  • 创建用于自动化通知渠道的智能工作流程。
  • 添加摘要功能，使最终用户的生活更加轻松
  • 在我们的应用内和系统中拥有新的用户偏好控制
  • 还有更多
  • 仍然是开源的！ 如果您还没有检查过 Novu，请随时访问我们的 Github 页面
    如果你能写信告诉我你在 Novu 中缺乏什么，我会很高兴，这样我就可以将它添加到我们的想法列表中。
    谢谢你。 没有你我们做不到！



原文:

Dima Grossman

Posted on May 11

Hi Community, We have exciting news to share!

@combarnea and I open-sourced the Novu project because we both felt the pain of building a notification infrastructure from scratch. I have written about the inception of the idea and you can read it here: Building the first open-source notification infrastructure.

Thanks to the incredible interest of this fantastic community, our Github project became trending and grew from 200 stars to 2.2K stars, and today has more than 3,500 stars.
We can’t thank the community enough.

What is Novu?

Novu is the first open-source notifications infrastructure for developers. It solves developers the pain of building from scratch and maintaining their notification systems. That can be an in-app notification center (like you might know from platforms like Facebook) to transactional messages sent over multiple delivery channels.

Our goal is to solve a large number of use-cases from digest engine, to advanced workflows management including user preferences, timezone awareness, and more.

And the best part is - We are building this with the incredible help and involvement of the open-source community. Together.

Lately, we have raised a $6.6m round. This means we can go and build this project full-time. A dream comes true. It will also enable us to grow it and become the de-facto solution for developers when thinking about notifications and product-based communication.

With our new funding, Novu recruited many of our contributors to work full-time on Novu and is now working on creating the best OSS notification infrastructure.

So what happened since the funding?

  • We have released our admin control panel for managing notifications (npx novu init) yay!.
  • We have created a new website, updated with all the new features.
  • We started to work closely with contributors and fund projects.
  • We have sponsored events.
  • Released our multi-environment support
  • Released our In-app notification center as an Iframe or as a React component
  • Released more providers and providers dashboard
  • Added New team collaboration and invites
  • Released a new monitor and observability page
  • Created an awesome registration flow using a CLI :)
  • All in open source!!!

The future?

  • Support every possible notification channel: Direct, Push and others.
  • Create an intelligent workflow for automating your notifications channels.
  • Add digest capabilities to make end-user life so much easier
  • Have a new user preference control within our in-app and as a system
  • Many more to come
  • Still all in open source!! :)

If you haven’t checked Novu yet, feel free to go to our Github page
https://github.com/novuhq/novu

Happy if you can write to me about what you lack in Novu so I can add it to our ideas list.
Thank you. We couldn’t do it without you!
